items.sourceNote: Vogue HK《低谷醫生》治癒金句整理，「先照顧今天的你」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《低谷醫生》有一句被反覆截圖的台詞：
「不要在意別人的目光，
先照顧好你自己。
今天的你要好好的，
才能幫助明天的自己。」

但低潮的人最常做的事，
剛好相反——

你不睡覺，
因為「再撐一下就熬過去了」；
你不吃飯，
因為「胃口不好就不要勉強」；
你不跟朋友說，
因為「不要把負能量倒給別人」。

結果你變成一個
從來不為自己存錢的人。
你把所有力氣都用來「撐過今天」，
沒留半點給明天的自己。

所以明天的你打開眼睛，
發現帳戶是空的，
根本沒有力氣再過一天。

照顧今天的自己，
不是奢侈，
是給明天的你預留下來的存款。

吃一頓正常的飯、
睡一場不滑手機的覺、
出去走一圈不戴耳機——
那不是浪費時間，
是匯款給明天那個還在等你的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是把自己擺到最後一個、最近又開始失眠的朋友——你不是在浪費時間，你是在匯款給明天的自己
variations: 進階版：今晚試一次——關手機、洗澡、好好睡。明天起床看看那個你，會不會比連續三天硬撐的版本，多一點點力氣

en.content:
A line from Doctor Slump that keeps getting screenshotted:
"Stop worrying about how others see you.
Take care of yourself first.
Today's you has to be okay
for tomorrow's you to have a chance."

But what people in a slump do
is the exact opposite —

You don't sleep,
because "just push through one more night."
You don't eat,
because "no appetite, don't force it."
You don't tell your friends,
because "don't dump negativity on people."

You turn into someone
who never saves anything for themselves.
You spend everything just surviving today,
leaving nothing for tomorrow's you.

So tomorrow's you opens their eyes,
finds the account empty,
and has nothing left to face another day.

Taking care of today's you
isn't a luxury.
It's a deposit you're leaving for tomorrow.

A real meal,
a full night of sleep without scrolling,
a walk outside with no earbuds —
that isn't wasted time.
It's a wire transfer to tomorrow's you,
who's still waiting for you to show up.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who always puts themselves last and just started losing sleep again — you're not wasting time, you're wiring funds to tomorrow's you
variations: Advanced: try it tonight — phone off, real shower, real sleep. Look at the version of you that wakes up tomorrow vs. the version that pushed through three nights raw. The difference is the receipt

items.sourceNote: POPBEE 2026治癒系韓劇台詞推薦，「你的幸福，為什麼要問別人？」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇有一句被瘋狂截圖的台詞：
「你的幸福，為什麼要問別人？」

但奇怪的是，
大多數人從來不問自己這個問題。

你會問朋友「我這份工作該不該辭？」
你會問家人「我這個對象適不適合？」
你會問IG的算命帳號「我什麼時候會遇到對的人？」

你問了一圈，
收到十種版本的答案，
結果還是回到原點：
你還是不知道自己要什麼。

那是因為從一開始，
答案就不在他們嘴裡。

他們可以告訴你他們的版本，
卻沒辦法替你過你的日子。
他們不會在你凌晨三點失眠的時候陪你坐著，
也不會在你決定錯了的時候替你承擔。

下一次想問別人之前，
先安靜地問自己一句：
「如果沒有人會評價我，
我真的想要哪一個？」

那個答案，才是你的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是徵詢一堆人意見、最後反而更迷惘的朋友——別人的版本永遠不會是你的人生
variations: 進階版：今天起把「我該怎麼辦」改成「我想要什麼」——前者讓別人主導，後者把方向盤拿回來

en.content:
A K-drama line that gets screenshotted on loop:
"Your happiness — why are you asking other people?"

The weird part is,
most people never ask themselves that.

You ask a friend, "Should I quit this job?"
You ask family, "Is this person right for me?"
You ask a tarot account on Instagram, "When will I meet the one?"

You collect ten different answers,
and end up exactly where you started:
still not knowing what you actually want.

That's because the answer
was never in their mouths to begin with.

They can tell you their version,
but they can't live your days for you.
They won't sit with you at 3 AM when you can't sleep,
or carry the weight when the choice turns out wrong.

Before you ask someone else next time,
ask yourself this quietly:
"If no one was going to judge me,
which one would I actually pick?"

That answer is yours.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who polls everyone and ends up more lost — other people's versions will never be your life
variations: Advanced: starting today, swap 'what should I do' for 'what do I want' — the first hands the wheel over, the second takes it back

items.sourceNote: KORB《戀愛的實用指南》2026年金句整理，「以為到了某個時間就能戀愛」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《戀愛的實用指南》有一句台詞戳到很多人：
「我以為時機到了，
隨時都可以開始談戀愛——
結果我把最好的那幾年，
全部浪費掉了。」

你二十三歲覺得「還太早，先拚事業」，
二十七歲覺得「還沒準備好，再等一下」，
三十一歲覺得「現在認真起來太累，隨緣」，
三十五歲突然發現：
你已經習慣一個人吃飯、一個人旅行、
一個人在家把熱湯喝完。

不是你不會愛人，
是你太久沒練習，
連「對某個人感興趣」都需要重新學。

戀愛從來不是一個按鈕，
按下去就會啟動。

它是一種肌肉。
你太久沒用它，
就會慢慢萎縮——
直到你想用的那一天，
才發現它不聽你的話。

所以那句話的意思不是叫你慌張地隨便愛，
是提醒你：
你不練習感受，
感受就會慢慢離你而去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是說「現在還不是時候」的朋友——感情這件事，越等越不會
variations: 進階版：今天先做小事——對一個你覺得有趣的人多回兩句話，那不是戀愛，那是讓那塊肌肉開始動的暖身

en.content:
A line from The Practical Guide to Love that lands hard:
"I thought I could fall in love
any time the timing felt right —
turns out I've already wasted
the best years on waiting."

At 23 you said "too early, focus on the career."
At 27 you said "not ready yet, give it a minute."
At 31 you said "too tired to start now, whatever happens happens."
At 35 you suddenly realize:
you've gotten used to eating alone,
traveling alone,
finishing a hot bowl of soup alone at home.

It's not that you can't love someone.
It's that you've been out of practice so long
even "being interested in a person"
feels like a skill you forgot.

Love was never a button
that starts when you press it.

It's a muscle.
Don't use it long enough,
and it slowly atrophies —
so the day you finally want to use it,
it stops listening to you.

That line isn't telling you to panic-love anyone.
It's a reminder:
if you don't practice feeling,
feeling slowly leaves you.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps saying 'not the right time' — this kind of muscle weakens the longer you wait
variations: Advanced: do something small today — reply two extra lines to someone interesting. That isn't love. That's the muscle starting to move again

items.sourceNote: Vogue HK《低谷醫生》「有悲傷的我就會有幸福的我」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《低谷醫生》有一句被反覆引用的台詞：
「雖然今天很幸福，
明天也可能會忽然變得悲傷，
但是沒關係——
有悲傷的我，就會有幸福的我。」

大多數人聽到「悲傷」這兩個字，
第一反應是想立刻把它趕走。

你會跟自己說：
「不要再難過了，
振作一點，
別人比你慘多了。」

但這句話真正在說的是：
悲傷不是一個你需要解決的故障。
悲傷是你還能感受的證據。

一個從不悲傷的人，
通常也不太會真正快樂——
他只是把所有開關都關掉了。

你願意為一段關係難過、
為一個夢想破碎心痛、
為一段日子的結束哭出來，
那都不是你的弱點。

那是你還沒被生活磨成一塊石頭的證據。

下次悲傷找上你的時候，
不要那麼急著趕它走。
它來，是來告訴你：
你還活著、還在乎、還沒放棄感受。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明很難過、卻一直跟自己說「我應該振作」的朋友——你不需要趕走悲傷，悲傷是你還活著的證明
variations: 進階版：今天試一次——感覺到悲傷時，不要立刻找事情分散注意力。坐下來，看它五分鐘。你會發現它其實沒有你想像的那麼可怕

en.content:
A line from Doctor Slump that keeps coming back:
"Today might be happy.
Tomorrow might suddenly turn sad.
But that's okay —
a me who can be sad
is also a me who can be happy."

Most people hear the word 'sad'
and immediately try to chase it away.

You tell yourself:
"Stop being upset.
Pull it together.
Other people have it worse."

But what that line is really saying:
sadness isn't a malfunction to fix.
Sadness is proof you can still feel.

A person who never feels sad
usually doesn't feel real joy either —
they just turned all the switches off.

The fact that you can grieve a relationship,
ache when a dream falls apart,
cry when a chapter ends —
that isn't weakness.

That's proof life hasn't worn you down into stone yet.

Next time sadness comes for you,
don't rush so hard to push it out.
It's here to tell you:
you're alive, you still care, you haven't stopped feeling.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps telling themselves 'I should pull it together' while clearly hurting — you don't need to evict sadness, it's the proof you're still alive
variations: Advanced: try this once — when sadness shows up, don't immediately distract yourself. Sit down, look at it for five minutes. You'll find it's smaller than it looked

items.sourceNote: 少女心文室韓劇台詞整理，「家裡有人等著你」溫暖主題延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一種被反覆寫進台詞的設定：
「不管在外面受了多少委屈，
只要想到家裡還有人等著你、
愛著你，
就好像又有力氣面對明天。」

但很多人看到這句話，
第一個感覺不是溫暖，
是空。

因為你下班回家，
打開門，
沒有人問你今天怎麼樣，
沒有人記得你昨天說過今天有重要會議，
甚至沒有一盞燈是替你留的。

但這句話真正想告訴你的，
從來不是「你必須有那個家」。

是：
你需要在你的人生裡，
建立一個地方——
回去的時候會被理解、被記得、被等著。

那個地方不一定是血緣，
可以是一個朋友的訊息、
一個固定週四的飯局、
一個會記得你不喜歡香菜的人。

所謂「家」，
從來不是一棟房子。

是這個世界上至少有一個座標，
可以讓你在崩潰的那一天，
知道往哪裡走。

如果你還沒有那個座標，
別放棄找——
找到一個，這個世界就不會那麼冷。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個過年回家覺得很累、平常下班又覺得房子很空的朋友——「家」不是地址，是有人記得你的座標
variations: 進階版：這週主動約一個讓你覺得安心的人吃一頓飯。不要找理由，就說「最近想見你」。那一頓飯，就是你在替自己造一個座標

en.content:
There's a setup K-dramas keep returning to:
"No matter how rough the world is to you,
the thought that someone's waiting at home,
someone loves you,
is what gives you strength for tomorrow."

A lot of people read that
and the first thing they feel isn't warmth.
It's emptiness.

Because you get home from work,
open the door,
and no one asks how today went.
No one remembered you said you had a big meeting yesterday.
Not even a single light was left on for you.

But what that line is actually saying
was never "you must have that family."

It's:
you need a place in your life —
somewhere you go back to
and are understood, remembered, waited for.

That place doesn't have to be blood.
It can be one friend's text thread,
a standing Thursday dinner,
someone who remembers you don't eat cilantro.

'Home' was never a building.

It's having at least one coordinate in this world
so that on the day you fall apart,
you know which direction to walk.

If you don't have that coordinate yet,
don't stop looking —
find one, and the world stops being so cold.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who feels exhausted going home for holidays and finds the apartment empty after work — 'home' isn't an address, it's a coordinate where someone remembers you
variations: Advanced: this week, message one person who makes you feel safe and ask them to dinner. No reason needed — just 'I wanted to see you.' That meal is you building a coordinate of your own

items.sourceNote: KORB《Sold Out On You》台詞整理，「相信命運就明天再見」浪漫場景延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇有一種讓人臉紅的台詞模板：
「如果你相信命運，
那我們明天，
就在這裡再見一次吧。」

聽起來很美，
但你仔細想會發現——
這句話真正的重點不是「命運」，
是那個人願意把明天的自己交出來。

「相信命運」是一種包裝，
核心其實是：
我願意冒一次險，
讓你決定我們有沒有以後。

現實裡比較常聽到的版本是：
「再說啦」、
「看狀況」、
「最近有點忙」。

沒有命運，
只有不夠想見你。

下次有人說「再聯絡」，
你就知道——
那不是命運在等你，
是對方還沒決定要不要把明天交給你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直在等對方主動聯絡、把「順其自然」當答案的朋友——「相信命運」常常是「我還沒決定」的禮貌說法
variations: 進階版：你不需要等命運。如果你想見一個人，就直接約一個時間、一個地點。命運會的事情，你自己也可以做

en.content:
K-dramas have this line that makes everyone blush:
"If you believe in fate,
let's meet here again tomorrow."

It sounds beautiful,
but think about it for a second —
the real point isn't 'fate.'
It's that the person is willing to hand over their tomorrow.

'Believing in fate' is the wrapping.
The core is:
I'm willing to take a risk
and let you decide if we have a future.

The real-world version usually sounds like:
"We'll see."
"Maybe later."
"Things are kind of busy."

There's no fate.
There's just 'not wanting to see you enough.'

Next time someone says 'let's keep in touch,'
you'll know —
that's not fate waiting on you.
It's the other person not yet deciding to give you their tomorrow.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps waiting for the other person to reach out, treating 'whatever happens, happens' as an answer — 'believing in fate' is often the polite version of 'I haven't decided yet'
variations: Advanced: you don't have to wait on fate. If you want to see someone, pick a time and a place. The things fate is supposed to do — you can do them yourself

items.sourceNote: POPBEE 2026治癒韓劇台詞推薦，「你的幸福為什麼要問別人」主題改寫

zh-tw.content:
有一句被反覆引用的韓劇台詞：
「你的幸福，
為什麼要問別人？」

第一次聽會覺得，
啊這句話好對。

第二次聽會發現，
你每天都在問。

買一件衣服要先問朋友適不適合、
離職前要先問家人會不會反對、
搬出去住前要先問男友能不能接受、
甚至連點哪一杯咖啡都要看店員臉色。

你以為你在「徵詢意見」，
其實你在「外包人生」。

問別人沒有錯，
錯的是你不問自己。

你連自己想要什麼都不知道，
別人怎麼可能告訴你你會不會幸福？

從今天開始，
做一個決定之前，
先問自己一句：
「如果沒有別人的意見，
我會選哪一個？」

那個答案，
才是你真正想要的人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個做決定永遠先問十個人意見、最後選了一個自己也不喜歡的選項的朋友——別人的意見可以參考，但你的人生不能外包
variations: 進階版：今天試一次，做一個小決定不要問任何人。買午餐、選電影、決定週末去哪——只用自己的答案。練習久了，大決定也會變得不那麼可怕

en.content:
There's a K-drama line people keep coming back to:
"Your happiness —
why are you asking other people about it?"

First time you hear it, you nod.
Yeah, that's right.

Second time, you realize
you ask other people every single day.

You ask a friend if the shirt suits you,
ask family before quitting your job,
ask your partner before moving out,
ask the barista with your eyes
before picking the coffee.

You think you're 'gathering input.'
You're actually outsourcing your life.

Asking isn't the problem.
Not asking yourself is.

If you don't know what you want,
how could anyone else
tell you whether you'll be happy?

Starting today,
before any decision,
ask yourself first:
"If no one had an opinion,
which would I pick?"

That answer
is the life you actually want.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who polls ten people for every decision and ends up choosing the option none of them — including themselves — actually wanted. Other people's input is fine, but you can't outsource your life
variations: Advanced: today, make one small decision without asking anyone. Lunch, movie, weekend plan — your answer only. Practice on small ones, and the big ones get less scary

items.sourceNote: Vogue HK《低谷醫生》16句金句整理，「現在不順不會一輩子不順」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《低谷醫生》裡有一句反覆出現的話：
「沒事的，
我們只是現在不順，
不會一輩子都不順。
抬頭挺胸，
很快就會有好事發生。」

你正在低潮的時候聽，
會覺得這句話有點輕。

畢竟你連起床都很難，
誰跟你講「抬頭挺胸」？

但這句話的關鍵，
從來不是「立刻好起來」，
是那個「現在」。

「現在」這個詞很重要，
因為它意味著有「以後」。

你現在不順，
不代表你的人生不順。
你現在很累，
不代表你接下來十年都會這麼累。
你現在愛的人沒有愛你，
不代表沒有人會愛你。

低潮的時候最容易犯的錯，
就是把「現在」當成「永遠」。

但季節都會換，
冬天再長都會結束，
你也是。

你不需要強迫自己抬頭挺胸，
你只需要記得：
這是「現在」，
不是「永遠」。

撐過這段，
你會發現自己其實一直在走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在低潮、覺得自己永遠走不出來的朋友——你只是「現在」不順，不是「永遠」不順，這兩個字差別很大
variations: 進階版：今晚睡前想一件三年前讓你覺得「我撐不過去了」的事——你撐過來了。那件事現在連名字都記不太清楚。三年後的你，看現在也會這樣

en.content:
In Doctor Slump, this line keeps coming back:
"It's okay.
We're just having a rough patch right now.
It's not going to last forever.
Keep your head up —
something good is coming."

When you're actually in the slump,
this line lands a little light.

Getting out of bed is already hard.
Who's got 'head up' energy?

But the real key in that line
isn't 'cheer up immediately.'
It's the words 'right now.'

'Right now' matters,
because it implies a 'later.'

You're struggling right now,
not for the rest of your life.
You're tired right now,
not for the next ten years.
The person you love right now doesn't love you back —
that doesn't mean no one will.

The most common mistake in a slump
is treating 'right now'
as 'forever.'

Seasons change.
Even the longest winter ends.
So do you.

You don't have to force yourself
to keep your head up.
You just have to remember:
this is 'right now,'
not 'forever.'

Get through this stretch
and you'll realize
you've been walking the whole time.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend in a slump who feels they'll never come out of it — it's only 'right now,' not 'forever.' Those two words make all the difference
variations: Advanced: tonight before bed, think of something three years ago that felt like 'I can't survive this' — you survived. You can barely remember the details now. Three years from now, you'll look at this the same way

items.sourceNote: TVBS女人我最大《今生也請多指教》15句台詞整理，「人到死都不會懂事」主題延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句被低估的台詞：
「人到死都不會懂事，
大家都是裝出來的。」

第一次聽會笑，
第二次聽會鬆一口氣。

因為你以為「長大」是會在某一天突然完成的事——
某天醒來，
你會懂得處理人際關係、
會自然管理財務、
會在家人吵架的時候從容打圓場、
會對所有事情都有答案。

但你三十歲了，
四十歲了，
還是經常覺得自己在裝。

你在公司裝有條理、
在朋友面前裝沒事、
在父母面前裝放心、
在另一半面前裝堅強。

你以為大家都已經懂了，
只有你還在裝。

但真相是：
所有人都在裝。

那個看起來最有條理的同事、
那個看起來婚姻很美滿的朋友、
那個看起來什麼都不怕的父母——
他們也都在裝，
只是裝得比較久，
所以裝得比較順。

所以下次你覺得「我怎麼還沒長大」，
記得這句話：
沒有人真的長大，
大家只是學會了，
在沒有準備好的時候，
還是把該做的事做完。

那不是裝，
那叫成熟。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個經常覺得「別人都很有條理只有我在亂」的朋友——所有人都在裝，差別只在於裝多久而已
variations: 進階版：下次有人在你面前看起來游刃有餘，記得他可能也是早上才哭過、出門前才崩潰過。你看到的是他選擇給你看的版本

en.content:
There's a K-drama line that doesn't get enough credit:
"Nobody actually grows up before they die.
Everyone's just pretending."

First time you hear it, you laugh.
Second time, you exhale.

Because you used to think
'becoming an adult' would happen on a specific day —
you'd wake up
knowing how to handle relationships,
manage money instinctively,
smoothly defuse family arguments,
have the answer to every question.

But you're thirty,
you're forty,
and you still feel like you're faking it.

Faking competence at work,
faking 'I'm fine' with friends,
faking 'don't worry about me' with parents,
faking strong with your partner.

You assume everyone else figured it out
and you're the only one still pretending.

The truth is:
everyone's pretending.

The coworker who looks the most put-together,
the friend whose marriage looks perfect,
the parents who seem afraid of nothing —
they're all pretending too.
They've just been pretending longer,
so they got smoother at it.

So next time you think
'why haven't I grown up yet,'
remember:
no one really grows up.
They just learn
to get the thing done
even when they weren't ready.

That's not pretending.
That's being an adult.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's convinced everyone has it together except them — everyone is faking it. The only difference is how long they've been doing it
variations: Advanced: next time someone looks like they have it all under control, remember — they might have cried that morning or fallen apart before walking out the door. You're seeing the version they chose to show you

items.sourceNote: POPBEE《淚之女王》10句經典台詞整理，「愛不是只有甜蜜，是一起撐過痛苦」主題延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《淚之女王》裡有一句很多人忽略的台詞：
「愛不是只有開心地說甜言蜜語，
愛是一起忍受痛苦，
是選擇留下來而不是跑掉。」

第一次聽會覺得，
好像有點沉。

但你想想就會發現，
所有看起來「很甜」的關係，
都是經歷過幾次「想跑」之後
還是選擇留下來的。

沒有人天生就會愛，
大家都是練習出來的：
練習在對方很煩的時候不爆走、
練習在自己很累的時候還願意聽他講話、
練習在吵架後先低頭、
練習在很想分手的那天又留下來吃一頓飯。

甜不是愛的證據，
「留下來」才是。

所以下次你又懷疑「我們是不是不夠愛了」，
問自己一個問題：
上一次很想跑的時候，
我為什麼留下來了？

那個答案，
就是愛還在的證明。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在懷疑自己感情是不是冷掉、開始覺得「不夠甜」的朋友——甜是浪漫，留下來才是愛
variations: 進階版：今晚問另一半一個問題：「你上一次很想離開的時候是什麼時候？」如果他願意誠實回答，那就是你們關係夠穩的證明——能談「想離開」的關係，才是真的留下來

en.content:
There's a line from Queen of Tears people often overlook:
"Love isn't just being happy and saying sweet things.
Love is enduring pain together.
It's choosing to stay
instead of running."

First time you hear it,
it sounds a little heavy.

But think about it for a second:
every relationship that looks 'so sweet'
is actually one where someone wanted to run
several times — and chose to stay.

No one is born knowing how to love.
Everyone practices:
practicing not exploding when they're annoying,
practicing still listening when you're exhausted,
practicing being the first to soften after a fight,
practicing one more dinner on the day you almost walked out.

Sweet isn't proof of love.
'Stayed' is.

So next time you wonder
'are we not in love anymore,'
ask yourself:
the last time I wanted to run,
why did I stay?

That answer
is the proof love is still there.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend questioning their relationship because it's not 'sweet enough' anymore — sweet is romance, but staying is love
variations: Advanced: tonight, ask your partner: 'When was the last time you wanted to leave?' If they answer honestly, that's proof the relationship is solid — a relationship where you can talk about 'wanting to leave' is one that actually stayed

items.sourceNote: 鬼怪經典台詞「跟你在一起的時光都很耀眼」整理，日常感官浪漫延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《鬼怪》裡有一句被引用到爛的台詞：
「跟你在一起的時光都很耀眼，
因為天氣好、
因為天氣不好、
因為天氣剛剛好——
每一天都很美好。」

聽起來很浪漫，
但你冷靜想想，
這句話真正厲害的地方，
不是天氣，
是「每一天」。

大部分的關係，
好的時候你覺得很愛、
累的時候你開始懷疑、
吵架的時候你想分開。

你的「愛」是有條件的，
要對方體貼、要天氣好、要心情對。

但這句台詞的設定是：
不管天氣怎樣、不管那一天發生什麼，
只要是「跟你」，
那一天就成立了。

這不是浪漫，
這叫做選擇。

你選擇了一個人之後，
你的好天氣壞天氣、
你的好心情壞心情，
都會因為他的存在而成立。

所以下次有人對你說
「跟你在一起好像每天都很好」，
那不是嘴甜，
那是他已經做完那個選擇了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在等「對的人會讓我每天都很開心」的朋友——對的人不是讓你每天都開心，是讓你每一天都「成立」
variations: 進階版：你最近的好天氣，是因為天氣，還是因為某個人？如果是後者，那個人值得你今晚發一則訊息——告訴他這件事，他不會知道，除非你說

en.content:
There's a Goblin line that's been quoted to death:
"Every moment with you was dazzling.
Because the weather was good.
Because the weather was bad.
Because the weather was just right.
Every single day was beautiful."

It sounds romantic,
but the real power of the line
isn't the weather.
It's 'every single day.'

In most relationships,
when things are good you feel in love,
when you're tired you start to doubt,
when you fight you want to leave.

Your love is conditional:
they have to be thoughtful,
the weather has to cooperate,
your mood has to be right.

But the line's premise is:
no matter the weather,
no matter what happens that day,
if it's 'with you,'
the day counts.

That's not romance.
That's a choice.

Once you've chosen someone,
your good and bad weather,
your good and bad moods,
all start to count
because they're in it.

So next time someone says
'every day with you feels good,'
that's not flattery.
They've already made the choice.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still waiting for 'the right person who'll make every day happy' — the right person doesn't make every day happy, they make every day count
variations: Advanced: was your good day today because of the weather, or because of someone? If it's the second one, that person deserves a text tonight — tell them. They won't know unless you say it

items.sourceNote: 《愛的迫降》「任何事拖久了都不好，無論是人還是愛情」經典台詞延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《愛的迫降》有一句被很多人記住的台詞：
「任何事拖久了都不好，
無論是人還是愛情都一樣。」

第一次聽會覺得，
有道理但有點殘忍。

第二次聽你會想到一個人。
那個你拖了三個月還沒回的訊息、
那個你拖了半年還沒確認的關係、
那個你拖了兩年還沒決定要不要在一起的對象。

你以為「再等等」是溫柔，
其實「再等等」是另一種傷害。

你拖著對方等一個你自己都不確定的答案，
你拖著自己等一個你早就知道結果的決定。

拖越久，
喜歡的會變成不甘心，
不甘心的會變成怨。

所以這句台詞真正在說的是：
如果你已經知道答案，
就不要拖。

答應或拒絕、
留下或離開、
說愛或說再見——
越早做完，傷害越小。

拖延不是溫柔，
是把痛苦分期付款給對方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明知不愛但還在拖、或是明知該離開但還在留的朋友——拖延不是善意，是把痛苦延長
variations: 進階版：今晚做一個決定——那個你已經想了三個月的答案，現在就傳出去。痛一次比痛三個月好

en.content:
There's a Crash Landing on You line a lot of people remember:
"Dragging anything out is bad —
whether it's a person
or a relationship."

First time you hear it,
it sounds true but a little brutal.

Second time, someone comes to mind.
The message you've left unread for three months.
The relationship you haven't defined in half a year.
The person you've been not-deciding about for two years.

You thought 'let me think a bit more' was kind.
It's actually another kind of damage.

You're making them wait on an answer
you aren't even sure of.
You're making yourself wait on a decision
you already know.

The longer you drag it,
the more 'like' becomes 'unwilling to let go,'
and 'unwilling to let go' becomes resentment.

What the line is really saying:
if you already know the answer,
don't drag it.

Yes or no.
Stay or go.
'I love you' or 'goodbye.'
The sooner you finish it,
the smaller the damage.

Dragging it out isn't being kind.
It's putting the other person on a pain payment plan.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who knows they don't love them but keeps dragging it out, or knows they should leave but keeps staying — dragging it isn't kindness, it's prolonging the pain
variations: Advanced: tonight, make a decision — the answer you've been holding for three months, send it now. Hurting once is better than hurting for three months

items.sourceNote: Beauty321「20句韓劇心動表白台詞」、「因為我們在一起完整了這個世界」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一種台詞最容易讓人融化：
「因為我們在一起，
才完整了這個世界。」

聽起來很浪漫，
但這句話被誤會了很久。

大部分人聽到「完整」就以為，
那個對的人會把你「補滿」——
你內心的缺口、你過去的傷、
你那些連自己都不喜歡的部分，
都會被他治好。

所以你拼命找那個能「補你」的人。
找了十年，
發現每個人最後都讓你更空。

但這句台詞的本意，
從來不是「他補你」、
也不是「你補他」。

是「一起，世界才完整」。

你還是不完整、
他也還是不完整——
但你們把彼此的不完整放在一起，
那個世界就剛剛好。

所以不要再等那個「完整你的人」了，
等下去你會錯過所有人。

你要找的是
那個願意跟你「一起不完整」的人。
他知道你有缺口、
你知道他有缺口，
你們不假裝、不修補，
就這樣放在一起。

那才是這句台詞真正的意思。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直在等「對的人會讓我變完整」的朋友——沒有人能讓你變完整，你只能找一個願意跟你一起不完整的人
variations: 進階版：今晚列三個你覺得自己「不夠好」的地方，然後想想——身邊有沒有一個人，知道這三件事還是留下來？如果有，那個人就是答案

en.content:
K-dramas have a kind of line that melts everyone:
"Because we're together,
the world became complete."

It sounds romantic,
but this line has been misunderstood for a long time.

Most people hear 'complete' and think
the right person will 'fill you in' —
your gaps, your past wounds,
the parts of yourself you don't like,
will all be healed by them.

So you spend years searching
for the person who'll 'complete' you.
Ten years later,
everyone has just left you emptier.

But the line never meant
'they complete you'
or 'you complete them.'

It meant 'together, the world becomes complete.'

You're still not whole.
They're still not whole.
But you put your incompleteness
next to theirs,
and somehow the world fits.

So stop waiting
for the person who'll 'make you whole.'
If you keep waiting, you'll miss everyone.

What you're looking for
is the person willing to be 'incomplete with you.'
They know you have gaps.
You know they have gaps.
No pretending, no fixing.
Just placed next to each other.

That's what the line actually means.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still waiting for 'the right person who'll make me whole' — no one can complete you. You can only find someone willing to be incomplete alongside you
variations: Advanced: tonight, list three things about yourself you think 'aren't good enough,' then ask — is there someone in your life who knows all three and still stayed? If yes, that's the answer

items.sourceNote: POPBEE《淚之女王》10句經典台詞整理，婚姻與愛情命題延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《淚之女王》裡有一個被很多人忽略的細節：
他們吵到最兇的那一場，
沒有人說「我要離婚」，
說的是「你還記不記得我們最一開始的樣子」。

這句話比所有甜言蜜語都殘忍。

因為「記不記得」的意思是，
我現在已經快不記得了。

婚姻裡最危險的，
從來不是大吵一架，
是某一天你發現——
你連對方原本喜歡你哪一點都想不起來。

甜的時候，
你以為「我愛你」是一句話；
累的時候，
你才知道「我愛你」是一個工作。

要每天上班、不能請假、
沒有年終、偶爾還要加班。

那些撐過去的人不是比較幸運，
是他們願意每天打卡。

所以下次你跟某個人說「我愛你」的時候，
你可以順便問問自己：
明天你還願意打卡嗎？
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在猶豫要不要繼續這段關係的朋友——愛不是感覺，是一份願不願意每天打卡的工作
variations: 進階版：今晚回想一下你跟對方「最一開始」的樣子，如果你還想得起來——恭喜你，這段關係還有救；如果想不起來，那也是一個答案

en.content:
There's a detail in Queen of Tears most people missed:
in their worst fight,
no one said "I want a divorce."
What they said was
"do you still remember
what we were like at the start?"

That line is more brutal than any insult.

Because "do you remember"
means
I'm already starting to forget.

The most dangerous thing in a marriage
isn't a huge fight.
It's the day you realize
you can't remember
what they originally liked about you.

When things are sweet,
you think "I love you" is a sentence.
When things are tired,
you learn that "I love you" is a job.

Daily clock-in. No PTO.
No year-end bonus.
Sometimes overtime.

The people who make it aren't luckier.
They just keep showing up to work.

So next time you say "I love you,"
ask yourself quietly:
are you still clocking in tomorrow?
en.editorNote: Send to the friend debating whether to stay in the relationship — love isn't a feeling, it's a job. The question is whether you're still clocking in tomorrow
variations: Advanced: tonight, try to remember what the two of you were like at the very start. If you can — good news, the relationship still has a chance. If you can't, that's also an answer

items.sourceNote: Tatler Asia〈2023年韓劇15大超寫實金句〉《絕世網紅》睡眠與順遂台詞延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
有一句韓劇台詞看似在罵人，
其實在點醒人：
「發生這點事就睡不著，
看來你之前過得還蠻順遂的。」

第一次聽你會生氣，
第二次聽你會沉默，
第三次聽你會笑出來——
因為你發現，是真的。

我們這代人最大的問題不是吃苦，
是「沒吃過苦」的人開始遇到苦。

小時候被保護得很好、
讀書的時候照著SOP走、
畢業前以為人生有一個固定的下一步。

結果某一天，
工作出包、感情出事、家裡出狀況——
你發現原來人生根本沒有SOP，
你才開始第一次失眠。

那個失眠不是因為事情有多大，
是因為你第一次發現「事情會失控」。

所以如果你最近睡不著，
不要罵自己玻璃心。
你只是在補上一堂
「世界本來就會出包」的課。

上完這堂課的人，
下一次遇到同樣的事，
會睡得著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個最近因為一件事失眠好幾天、開始懷疑自己抗壓性的朋友——失眠不是脆弱，是在補課
variations: 進階版：你最近睡不著的那件事，五年後回頭看會是大事還是小事？如果是小事，那今晚就睡吧——你現在的痛，是因為你還沒學會這件事很小

en.content:
There's a K-drama line that sounds like an insult
but is actually a wake-up call:
"You lost sleep over this?
Sounds like life's been pretty smooth for you so far."

First time you hear it, you're offended.
Second time, you go quiet.
Third time, you laugh —
because you realize it's true.

The biggest problem with our generation
isn't that we suffered.
It's that people who never suffered
are meeting suffering for the first time.

Well-protected as kids.
Followed the SOP through school.
Graduated thinking life had a clear next step.

Then one day —
work goes wrong, relationship breaks,
something happens at home.
You realize life has no SOP.
And you have your first sleepless night.

The insomnia isn't because the thing is huge.
It's because you're learning, for the first time,
that things can spiral.

So if you can't sleep lately,
don't call yourself weak.
You're just taking a class
you missed earlier:
"things go wrong by default."

The people who pass this class
sleep through it next time.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's lost sleep for days over one thing and is now doubting their resilience — insomnia isn't fragility, it's catching up on a missed class
variations: Advanced: the thing keeping you up — will it look big or small five years from now? If small, go to sleep tonight. Your pain comes from not yet knowing how small it is

items.sourceNote: Pinkvilla〈10 K-drama quotes we live by〉《我的出走日記》解放與崇拜命題延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《我的出走日記》裡有一句被很多人抄在筆記本的話：
「我從來沒有真正活過。
我只是不斷地在拒絕。
從沒愛過、也沒被真正愛過——
所以我想，至少崇拜一個人試試看。」

看起來很喪，
其實在說一件很狠的事。

大部分人活到三十歲，
都不是「沒選過自己想要的人生」，
是「一直在拒絕不想要的」。

不想要這份工作、
不想要那段關係、
不想要那種生活——
你以為這些拒絕加在一起會變成一個答案，
結果只是把生活刪到剩下一片空白。

你沒有過自己的人生，
你只是過了一個「不是別人版本」的人生。

所以這句台詞真正在問的是：
你下次說「我不想要這個」的時候，
後面那句「那我想要什麼」，
你有答案嗎？

如果還沒有，
也沒關係——
那句台詞的角色
也是從「崇拜一個人」開始找答案的。

你可以從一件小事開始崇拜：
一個你欣賞的朋友、
一個你佩服的同事、
一個你覺得活得有重量的陌生人。

崇拜不是依賴，
是讓你看見「原來人可以那樣活」。
看見了，你才知道自己也可以。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直在拒絕、卻說不出自己要什麼的朋友——你不用馬上找到答案，你只需要先找到一個你想成為的樣子
variations: 進階版：今晚想一個你最近覺得「活得很扎實」的人，不用很厲害的人，只要他讓你覺得「我也想要這樣」——那個人就是你的下一個方向

en.content:
There's a line from My Liberation Notes
that people keep writing in their notebooks:
"I've never really lived.
I've just been refusing.
Never loved, never truly been loved —
so I thought, let me at least try worshipping someone."

It sounds bleak.
It's actually saying something brutal.

Most people at thirty
haven't 'chosen the life they wanted.'
They've just been refusing
the lives they didn't want.

Don't want this job.
Don't want that relationship.
Don't want that kind of life.
You thought stacking up refusals
would give you an answer.
It just edited your life down
to a blank page.

You didn't live your life.
You lived a 'not-someone-else's-version' life.

So what the line is really asking:
next time you say 'I don't want this,'
do you have an answer
to 'then what do I want'?

If you don't, that's okay too.
The character in that line
also started by 'worshipping someone'
to find the answer.

You can start small.
Worship a friend you admire.
A coworker you respect.
A stranger who seems to live with weight.

Worship isn't dependence.
It's letting yourself see
that 'a person can live that way.'
Once you see it,
you know you can too.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps refusing things but can't say what they actually want — you don't need the answer yet, you just need someone who shows you a version of life you'd want
variations: Advanced: tonight, think of one person you've recently felt 'lives with real weight.' Not someone famous, just someone who makes you think 'I'd want that too.' That person is your next direction

items.sourceNote: KORB〈70+ Quotes The Practical Guide To Love 2026〉現代戀愛開場改寫

zh-tw.content:
《戀愛實用指南》一開場就講了一句很狠的話：
「美麗的愛情故事，
總是從一場宿命般的相遇開始。
但現在的單身的人，
已經不等命運來找他們了。
他們自己挑對象，
自己決定開始的時間，
自己決定結束的時間。」

聽起來很冷靜，
其實在揭穿一件事：
我們以為自己「不相信宿命」，
其實只是「不想等」。

等很累，
等到的東西也不一定是想要的。
所以乾脆自己安排——
自己滑、自己約、自己決定。

結果你發現，
效率變高了，
感覺變淡了。

因為宿命之所以動人，
不是因為它準，
是因為它「不是你選的」。

當你開始什麼都自己選，
你也順便把「驚喜」這個選項刪掉了。

不是叫你回去等緣分，
是提醒你：
下次遇到一個讓你心動的人，
不要急著把他塞進你的時程表。

留一點不確定，
戀愛才有空氣可以呼吸。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個把交友App當成購物清單在用、最近開始覺得「沒什麼感覺」的朋友——效率殺死的不只是時間
variations: 進階版：你最近一次心動是什麼時候？如果想不起來，可能不是你沒遇到，是你太快把每個遇到的人都「分類」掉了

en.content:
The Practical Guide to Love opens with a brutal line:
"A beautiful love story
always begins with a fateful encounter.
But singles these days
don't wait around for fate.
They choose their partners however they like,
decide when to start,
decide when to end."

It sounds calm.
It's actually exposing something:
we think we 'don't believe in fate.'
We just don't want to wait.

Waiting is exhausting.
And what you wait for
isn't always what you wanted.
So we manage it ourselves —
swipe, schedule, decide.

Then you notice:
efficiency went up.
Feeling went down.

Because fate is moving
not because it's accurate,
but because 'you didn't choose it.'

The moment you start choosing everything,
you quietly delete
the 'surprise' option too.

Not telling you to wait for destiny.
Just reminding you:
next time someone makes your heart move,
don't rush to slot them into your calendar.

Leave a little uncertainty.
Love needs air to breathe.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend treating dating apps like a shopping list who's starting to feel nothing — efficiency kills more than just time
variations: Advanced: when was the last time your heart actually moved? If you can't remember, it might not be that no one's out there — it might be that you're categorizing them too fast

items.sourceNote: POPBEE〈2026 必看治癒系韓劇台詞〉幸福不假外求主題延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句被截圖瘋傳的話：
「你的幸福，
為什麼要去問別人？」

第一次看你覺得是廢話，
第二次看你會卡住。

因為你發現——
你真的在問別人。

買了一件衣服，
要拍照問朋友好不好看。
決定要不要分手，
要先問三個閨密的意見。
升遷選擇要不要接，
要先看別人會不會羨慕。

你不是不知道自己想要什麼，
你是不敢「只用自己的標準」就決定。

因為一旦只用自己的標準，
後悔的時候就沒有人可以怪。

所以這句台詞真正在說的是：
你害怕的不是做錯選擇，
你害怕的是「一個人承擔」。

但所有的幸福，
本來就是一個人的事。
別人可以陪你開心、陪你難過，
但沒有人可以幫你「感覺到幸福」。

你的幸福，
只有你自己知道。
所以從今天開始，
買那件衣服之前先少問一個人，
做那個決定之前先少看一個臉色。

你會發現：
你的幸福，
本來就不需要評審團。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每件事都要問十個人意見、最後還是不開心的朋友——幸福沒有匿名投票
variations: 進階版：今天做一個小決定，不要問任何人，看自己會不會後悔——這就是你練習「自己定義幸福」的第一步

en.content:
There's a screenshot going around
of a K-drama line:
"Your happiness —
why are you asking other people about it?"

First time you read it, you think it's obvious.
Second time, you freeze.

Because you realize —
you actually do.

Buy a shirt,
take a photo, send it to a friend.
Thinking about breaking up,
run it past three close friends first.
Deciding about a promotion,
check whether others would envy it.

It's not that you don't know what you want.
It's that you're scared to decide
on your own standard alone.

Because once it's just your standard,
there's no one to blame later.

So what this line is really saying:
you're not afraid of choosing wrong.
You're afraid of carrying it alone.

But happiness was always solo work.
Others can sit with you in joy or sadness,
but no one can feel happy on your behalf.

Your happiness,
only you can know.
Starting today,
ask one fewer person before buying that shirt,
check one fewer face before making that call.

You'll notice:
your happiness
never needed a panel of judges.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who asks ten people about every decision and still ends up unhappy — happiness doesn't take anonymous votes
variations: Advanced: make one small decision today without asking anyone. See if you regret it. That's step one of learning to define happiness yourself

items.sourceNote: Perpetually in Transit〈Famous KDrama Quotes〉《我的大叔》朴東勳「證明自己」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《我的大叔》朴東勳有一段話，
看完會讓你安靜很久：
「每個人一輩子都在拚命想擁有東西。
所以花了一輩子
要向所有人證明自己。
但其實沒有人真的知道，
自己這樣做
到底得到了什麼。」

你二十歲覺得這段話很無聊，
你三十歲開始懂，
你三十五歲會發現——
他在說你。

買車不是為了開，
是為了停在同學會的停車場。
換房子不是為了住得舒服，
是為了過年回家可以講「我換房了」。
升遷不是為了喜歡那份工作，
是為了在LinkedIn上多一行頭銜。

我們每個人都在偷偷比賽，
但從來沒人說明這個比賽要比什麼、
冠軍會得到什麼。

比到最後你才發現：
沒有評審、沒有獎盃、沒有閉幕式。

你拿了一堆東西去證明自己，
但「自己」一直沒有出席。

所以這段台詞的真正意思是：
停下來問一句——
如果今天沒有人在看，
你還會買那台車嗎？
你還會換那間房嗎？
你還會接那個職位嗎？

會的事情，繼續。
不會的事情，
那就是你一直在演給別人看的人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個最近換車、換房、換工作但越來越不快樂的朋友——這段話他需要在一個沒人打擾的晚上慢慢看
variations: 進階版：今天列出你最近三個「最得意」的決定，每個後面寫「如果沒人知道我還會做嗎」——這個練習會很痛，但會很有用

en.content:
There's a Park Dong-hoon line in My Mister
that will keep you quiet for a while:
"Everyone spends their whole life
desperate to own things.
So they spend their whole life
trying to prove themselves to everyone.
But nobody actually knows
what they're gaining
by doing all of this."

At twenty you find this boring.
At thirty you start to get it.
At thirty-five you realize —
he's describing you.

The car isn't bought to drive.
It's parked at the reunion.
The new place isn't for comfort.
It's for the line 'we just moved' at family dinner.
The promotion isn't for the work.
It's for the extra line on LinkedIn.

We're all in a secret competition.
No one explained what the contest measures
or what the winner gets.

By the end you notice:
no judges, no trophy, no closing ceremony.

You collected things to prove yourself,
but 'yourself' never showed up.

So what the line actually means:
pause and ask —
if no one was watching today,
would you still buy that car?
Would you still move into that place?
Would you still take that title?

If yes, continue.
If no,
that's the life you've been performing
for everyone but you.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who recently upgraded car, home, or job and is somehow less happy — this needs to be read slowly on a quiet night
variations: Advanced: list the three decisions you're most proud of recently. After each one write 'would I still do this if no one knew.' This exercise hurts, but it works

items.sourceNote: Soompi〈10 K-Drama Lines To Lift Your Spirits〉接受生活淋雨概念延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句很多人忽略的台詞：
「人生本來就會遇到意料之外的事。
就算撐著傘，
你還是會被淋濕。
那就把手舉起來，
好好迎接這場雨。」

聽起來很文青，
其實在講一個很實用的觀念：
你不是淋濕了，
你是「以為自己不會淋濕」。

人生會發生的事，
你的傘擋不住一半。
失業會來、生病會來、
感情會壞、家人會老、
計畫好的事情會被取消。

你撐傘是想擋雨，
但雨本來就不是用擋的。

所以這句台詞真正在說：
你之所以痛苦，
不是因為下雨，
是因為你一直在跟「下雨」這件事生氣。

承認下雨，
不是放棄。
是把力氣從「對抗」轉到「應對」。

對抗會累，
應對會成長。

下次又出事的時候，
先深呼吸三秒，
跟自己說一句：
「啊，這個我之前就知道會來，
只是不知道哪天。」

然後把手舉起來，
看看這場雨
會帶你去哪裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個最近覺得「為什麼倒楣的都是我」的朋友——不是針對你，是你把雨當成攻擊
variations: 進階版：今天遇到一件不順的事，先不要罵、不要解決，先只是「看著它」三十秒——你會發現它沒你想得那麼大

en.content:
There's a K-drama line most people skim past:
"You're bound to meet unexpected things in life.
Even with an umbrella,
you'll still get drenched.
So just put your hands up
and welcome the rain."

It sounds poetic.
It's actually saying something practical:
you're not soaked because it rained.
You're soaked because you assumed it wouldn't.

Life stuff your umbrella can't block:
layoffs, illness,
relationships breaking, parents aging,
the plan you spent months on getting cancelled.

You hold an umbrella to block rain,
but rain was never something you block.

So what the line really means:
your pain isn't from the rain.
It's from staying angry at the fact that it rains.

Admitting it rains
isn't giving up.
It's moving your energy
from 'fighting' to 'responding.'

Fighting exhausts you.
Responding grows you.

Next time something goes wrong,
breathe for three seconds first
and tell yourself:
"Right, I knew this would come.
I just didn't know which day."

Then put your hands up
and see
where this rain takes you.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps asking 'why does this always happen to me' — it's not personal, you're just treating rain as an attack
variations: Advanced: when something annoying happens today, don't react and don't fix it. Just look at it for thirty seconds. You'll notice it's smaller than you thought

items.sourceNote: KORB〈70+ Quotes The Practical Guide To Love 2026〉「每個人都是一個宇宙」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《戀愛的實用指南》裡有一句很多人路過沒注意：
「相親見了那麼多人之後我才發現，
世界很大，每個人都不一樣，
每一個人都是一個獨立的宇宙。」

聽起來像是文藝句，
其實是在講一件很實際的事：
你以為自己一直沒遇到對的人，
其實是你一直在用同一套標準，
篩掉所有不一樣的人。

你滑交友軟體三秒就右滑，
你約會三十分鐘就決定不繼續，
你聊兩句就在心裡判他出局——
你不是在認識人，
你是在驗證自己腦袋裡那個「對的人」長怎樣。

但那個「對的人」是你自己編的，
不是真的會出現在現實裡的人。

真正會留在你身邊的那個人，
通常不會完全符合清單，
但會在某個瞬間
讓你願意把清單收起來。

所以這句台詞真正在說：
別急著刷掉別人，
每個人都是一個宇宙——
你只是還沒有走進去看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直抱怨「沒有適合的對象」、但每次約會三十分鐘就放棄的朋友——可能不是沒有人，是你太快下判斷
variations: 進階版：下次約會前，把你心中那張「對的人清單」先放在抽屜裡，整場約會只做一件事——好奇對方

en.content:
There's a line in The Practical Guide To Love
that most people skim past:
"After all those blind dates I realized
the world is big, everyone is different,
and each person is their own universe."

It sounds like a poetic throwaway.
It's actually saying something practical:
you think you keep failing to meet the right person.
Really you keep using the same checklist
to screen out everyone who's different.

Three seconds on the app, swipe left.
Thirty minutes into the date, mentally checked out.
Two messages in, already disqualified —
you're not meeting people,
you're verifying the imaginary person
living in your head.

But that 'right person' is something you wrote.
They're not actually walking around out there.

The one who ends up staying
usually doesn't fit the checklist.
They just make you want
to fold the checklist away.

So what the line really means:
stop swiping people out so fast.
Each person is a universe —
you just haven't walked in to look.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps complaining 'no one's right' but checks out thirty minutes into every date — maybe it's not the people, it's how fast you're judging
variations: Advanced: before your next date, put your mental checklist in a drawer. Spend the whole date doing one thing — being curious about them

items.sourceNote: Vogue HK《低谷醫生》16句金句整理，「不要在意別人的目光，先照顧今天的你」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《低谷醫生》有一句台詞，
聽完會讓你想關掉社群一個月：
「不要在意別人的目光，
先照顧好你自己。
今天的你要好好的，
才能去幫到明天的自己。」

你以為這是雞湯，
其實是在罵你：
你大部分的累，
不是來自工作、不是來自人際，
是來自你一直在偷偷觀察別人怎麼看你。

發限動會檢查誰看了、
講話會回想剛剛是不是太突兀、
貼一張照片會比對留言數、
吃個飯會想等等發限動該配什麼字。

你的腦袋有一半的算力，
都在處理「別人怎麼看我」。
剩下一半才在過日子。

難怪會累。

所以這句台詞真正在說：
你今天有沒有好好吃飯、
有沒有好好睡覺、
有沒有好好對自己說一句話——
比所有人怎麼看你重要一百倍。

別人怎麼想，
是別人的工作。

你今天還活得好好的，
才是你的工作。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一發文就盯著數字、別人沒按讚就懷疑自己的朋友——把那部分腦袋的算力留給自己
variations: 進階版：今天做一件「沒人會知道」的事——不發限動、不傳給朋友、不拍照——只為了你自己。看看那種感覺有多陌生

en.content:
There's a line in Doctor Slump
that will make you want to close social media for a month:
"Don't worry about how others see you.
Take care of yourself first.
Today's you needs to be okay
to help tomorrow's you."

You think it's a self-help line.
It's actually calling you out:
most of your tiredness
doesn't come from work or from people.
It comes from quietly tracking
how everyone else sees you.

Post a story, check who watched.
Finish a sentence, replay it for awkwardness.
Upload a photo, compare the like count.
Eat a meal, plan the caption for it.

Half your mental processing power
is running 'how do I look to others.'
The other half is actually living.

No wonder you're tired.

So what the line really means:
did you eat properly today,
sleep properly today,
say one kind thing to yourself today —
that matters a hundred times more
than how anyone perceives you.

What they think
is their job.

Keeping you alive and okay today
is yours.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who watches the like count and spirals when no one engages — pull that processing power back for yourself
variations: Advanced: do one thing today that nobody will know about — no story, no group chat, no photo. Just for you. Notice how unfamiliar that feels

items.sourceNote: TVBS女人我最大《今生也請多指教》15句台詞整理，「不要被動等待，主動去爭取」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《今生也請多指教》有一句很短，
但很多人需要重看三次的台詞：
「不要被動等待，
主動去爭取吧。」

聽起來是廢話，
仔細想會發現你最近做的很多事，
其實都是「等」：
等對方先開口、
等老闆主動加薪、
等狀態好一點再開始、
等存到一筆錢再做那件事、
等別人證明值得你付出、
等一個沒有風險的時機。

你以為自己在「準備」，
其實是在「拖」。
你以為自己在「觀望」，
其實是在「等別人替你決定」。

等的問題不是花時間，
是把人生的決定權
交到你不認識的人或事手上。

別人不會替你升職、
別人不會替你告白、
別人不會替你訂機票、
別人不會替你結束那段消耗你的關係。

所以這句台詞真正在說：
你最近一直在等的那件事，
試試看今天主動踏出一步——
哪怕只是寄一封信、
打一通電話、
按下那個你猶豫了三個月的按鈕。

人生不會給「等的人」獎品，
只會給「動的人」答案。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個說「等時機到了我就會去做」、結果三年都還在等的朋友——時機不會來，動作會帶你去那裡
variations: 進階版：今天列出三件你「在等」的事，每一件都做一個最小的動作——寄一封信、查一個資料、講一句話。你會發現「等」其實是你自己卡住了

en.content:
There's a short line in See You In My 19th Life
that people need to rewatch three times:
"Don't wait passively.
Go after it."

Sounds like a throwaway.
Look closer and you'll see
most of what you've been doing lately
is waiting:
waiting for them to message first,
waiting for your boss to offer the raise,
waiting until you feel ready,
waiting until you've saved enough,
waiting for the other person to prove they're worth it,
waiting for a risk-free moment.

You think you're 'preparing.'
You're stalling.
You think you're 'observing.'
You're handing the decision
to someone who doesn't know you exist.

The problem with waiting isn't the time.
It's that you've handed the steering wheel
to people and circumstances
that have no plan for you.

No one will promote you for you.
No one will confess for you.
No one will book the flight for you.
No one will end the relationship
that's draining you.

So what the line really means:
that thing you've been waiting on —
take one small step today.
Send the email,
make the call,
click the button you've been hovering over
for three months.

Life doesn't give prizes to people who wait.
It gives answers to people who move.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who says 'I'll do it when the time is right' and three years later is still waiting — the time isn't coming, the motion brings you there
variations: Advanced: list three things you're 'waiting on' today. Take the smallest possible action on each — one email, one search, one sentence. You'll see waiting was just you, stuck

items.sourceNote: POPBEE《淚之女王》10句經典台詞整理，「你是我唯一看見的人，其他都模糊了」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《淚之女王》有一句很多人收藏卻說不清楚為什麼的台詞：
「在那個瞬間，
我眼裡只看得到你，
其他的一切都變模糊了。」

聽起來是浪漫話，
其實是一個非常具體的訊號：
你是不是「對的人」，
身體會比你的腦袋先知道。

人很奇怪，
在一群人裡面，
你的視線會自動鎖定某個人——
他講話你會分外仔細聽、
他靠近你會偷偷感覺到、
他離開那個空間，
整個房間的亮度好像都掉一階。

這不是想像，
這是身體在替你做選擇。

問題是大部分人會把這個訊號蓋掉，
用「他不夠優秀」、
「他現在沒空」、
「我不該喜歡他」
這些理由蓋掉自己的直覺，
然後過幾年才後悔——
「其實那時候我就知道。」

所以這句台詞真正在說：
下次你發現自己在某個人身上「對焦」了，
不要急著用理由把那個感覺蓋掉。

你的身體看得比你清楚。
讓它說一次話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是用「不適合」當理由、但其實心裡早就有答案的朋友——身體比腦袋誠實
variations: 進階版：回想最近三個月，你在哪個人身上「自動對焦」過？那個瞬間你做了什麼？如果什麼都沒做，問問自己為什麼

en.content:
There's a line from Queen of Tears
that people save without knowing why:
"In that moment,
you were the only one I could see.
Everything else blurred."

Sounds romantic.
It's actually a very specific signal:
whether someone is 'the one'
is something your body knows
before your brain catches up.

People are strange.
In a room full of people,
your eyes auto-lock on one person —
you listen harder when they speak,
you can feel them step closer,
and when they leave the room
the lighting somehow drops a notch.

That's not your imagination.
That's your body deciding for you.

The problem is most people override the signal
with 'he's not impressive enough,'
'she's not available right now,'
'I shouldn't like him,'
and bury the instinct under reasons.
Then years later they regret it:
'I knew, back then.'

So what the line really means:
next time you find yourself auto-focusing on someone,
don't rush to bury the feeling under logic.

Your body sees clearer than you do.
Let it speak once.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who uses 'not compatible' as a default reason but already knows the answer — the body is more honest than the brain
variations: Advanced: think back three months. Whose face did your eyes auto-focus on? What did you do in that moment? If nothing, ask yourself why

items.sourceNote: TALKace《莎拉的真偽人生》金句TOP10整理，「人們往往認為自己相信的就是真相」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《莎拉的真偽人生》有一句話讓很多人在看完後沉默很久：
「人們往往，
會把自己想相信的，
當成真相。」

聽起來像在說那個女主角，
其實是在說螢幕前的你。

你以為他「只是最近比較忙」，
你以為公司「下個月就會發獎金了」，
你以為那個朋友「不是真的那個意思」，
你以為你「只是還沒準備好，不是不想做」。

這些不是觀察，
是你選擇要相信的版本。

大腦很厲害，
它會自動幫你過濾掉不舒服的資訊，
只留下能讓你繼續活下去的那一面。

問題不是被騙，
問題是你自願走進那個版本，
然後反過來指責現實怎麼跟你想的不一樣。

所以這句台詞真正在說：
下次你覺得「事情應該是這樣」的時候，
停一秒問自己——
這是真相，
還是我希望它是真相？

兩者差很多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明知道對方在敷衍、卻還在自我說服的朋友——這句他需要聽見
variations: 進階版：列出你最近三件「我覺得應該會……」的事，然後在每一條後面加一句「但有什麼證據？」——你會看見大半是你自己畫的

en.content:
There's a line from The Art of Sarah
that leaves people quiet long after the episode ends:
"People tend to take
what they want to believe
and call it the truth."

Sounds like it's about the lead character.
It's about you, watching.

You think he's 'just been busy lately.'
You think the company will 'definitely pay the bonus next month.'
You think your friend 'didn't really mean it that way.'
You think you're 'not ready yet, not unwilling.'

These aren't observations.
They're versions you chose to believe.

The brain is impressive.
It filters out the uncomfortable signals
and leaves only the version that lets you keep going.

The problem isn't being lied to.
The problem is you walked into that version voluntarily
and now blame reality
for not matching it.

So what the line really means:
next time you catch yourself thinking 'it should be this way,'
pause for one second and ask —
is this the truth,
or is this what I want the truth to be?

The gap is huge.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who knows the other person is making excuses but keeps spinning their own version — they need this one
variations: Advanced: list three recent 'I assume it'll work out…' beliefs. Add 'but what's my evidence?' to each. You'll see most of them are scenery you painted yourself

items.sourceNote: GirlStyle《莎拉的真偽人生》十大靈魂金句，「標籤夠華麗，就沒人在意內容」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《莎拉的真偽人生》有一句話戳到了2026年所有人：
「標籤只要夠華麗，
就不會有人在意裡面裝什麼。」

這不是在罵那個女主角，
這是在描述你的IG首頁。

你發限動，會挑角度；
你po貼文，會修字體；
你寫bio，會偷偷寫得比你本人厲害一點點。

不是你虛榮，
是這個世代訓練我們相信——
包裝是入場券，
沒有包裝就沒有人看見你的內容。

但久了會發生一件可怕的事：
你開始花80%的力氣在包裝，
只剩20%在實際做事，
然後有一天你發現，
你比較像是一個帳號，
比較不像一個人。

所以這句台詞真正的提醒是：
標籤是給別人看的，
內容是你自己要過的。

下次拍照前停一秒，
問自己——
如果這一刻不能發任何地方，
我還會這樣做嗎？

答案是「不會」的事，
通常都是你在演給陌生人看的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個發限動花一小時、但已經一個月沒好好吃一頓飯的朋友——這句他現在最需要
variations: 進階版：今天故意做一件「不能發到任何地方」的事，看看你會不會覺得「白做了」——那個感覺就是你被標籤綁架的程度

en.content:
There's a line from The Art of Sarah
that hits every 2026 viewer in the chest:
"If the label is dazzling enough,
no one looks at what's inside."

This isn't about the lead character.
This is your Instagram grid.

You post a story — you pick the angle.
You write a caption — you tweak the font.
You edit your bio so the version on screen
is a small upgrade on the version you are.

It's not that you're vain.
The era trained us to believe
packaging is the entry fee,
and without it no one sees your content.

But a strange thing happens over time:
you start spending 80% of your energy on packaging
and 20% on actually doing things,
and one day you notice
you resemble an account
more than a person.

So what the line really means:
the label is for them.
The content is what you have to live inside.

Before your next photo, pause one second and ask —
if I couldn't post this anywhere,
would I still be doing it?

The things you answer 'no' to
are usually the ones you're performing
for strangers.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who spends an hour on a single story but hasn't sat down to a real meal in a month — they need this one now
variations: Advanced: do one thing today that you deliberately can't post anywhere. Notice if you feel it was 'wasted' — that feeling is the size of the label's grip on you

items.sourceNote: POPBEE 2026治癒系韓劇台詞推薦，「不需要每天都過得轟轟烈烈，收集微小快樂」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
2026年最被收藏的韓劇治癒金句之一是這樣的：
「你不需要每天都過得轟轟烈烈，
只要每天收集到幾個
微小的快樂瞬間，
就夠了。」

聽起來像雞湯，
其實是一個非常具體的人生策略。

我們從小被教育要追求「大幸福」——
升學、升職、結婚、買房，
好像非要有一個「人生大事」發生，
那一天才算數。

結果你三十歲、三十五歲、四十歲，
回頭一看，
大幸福一年發生一次，
你卻把另外364天
當成「在等」的日子。

等於用一天，
換掉一年。
划不來。

真正讓人撐過長期的，
從來不是大幸福，
是那些被你忽略的小東西——
早上第一口熱咖啡、
陽光斜斜照進窗戶的那兩分鐘、
朋友傳來一個莫名其妙的迷因、
洗完澡之後乾淨的床單。

這些不是「將就」，
這些是你真正在生活的證據。

所以這句台詞真正在說：
與其等那個會改變一切的時刻，
不如先學會看見今天已經有的那些光。

大事很少。
小事每天都有。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總在等「人生大事」、卻覺得每天都很空的朋友——這句他需要從今天就開始實作
variations: 進階版：今天睡前，寫下三件今天讓你「微微開心」的小事——連續做三十天，你會發現你的生活其實比你以為的滿

en.content:
One of the most-saved healing K-drama lines of 2026 goes:
"You don't have to live every day
in grand strokes.
If you can collect a few small moments of joy each day,
that's already enough."

Sounds like a comfort quote.
It's actually a very specific life strategy.

We were raised to chase the big things —
the acceptance letter, the promotion, the wedding, the house —
as if a day only counts
when something 'major' happens.

So you hit thirty, thirty-five, forty,
look back,
and realize the big happiness arrives once a year
while you spent the other 364 days
'waiting.'

You traded a year for a day.
Bad math.

What actually carries people through long stretches
is never the big happiness.
It's the small things you keep dismissing —
the first hot sip of morning coffee,
the two minutes of sun cutting across the floor,
a ridiculous meme from a friend,
clean sheets after a shower.

These aren't 'settling.'
These are the proof you're actually alive.

So what the line really means:
stop waiting for the moment that will change everything.
Learn to see the light already in today.

Big things are rare.
Small things happen every single day.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's always waiting for the 'big life event' but feels empty every other day — they can start practicing this one tonight
variations: Advanced: tonight, write down three small things from today that made you 'mildly happy.' Do it for thirty days. You'll notice your life is fuller than you thought

items.sourceNote: Vogue HK《低谷醫生》16句治癒金句，「不要在意別人的目光，先照顧你自己」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《低谷醫生》有一句話讓很多人截圖收藏：
「不要太在意別人的目光，
先照顧好你自己。
今天的你要好好的，
才能幫助明天的你。」

聽起來像一句安慰的話，
其實是一個很硬的提醒。

大部分人在低潮的時候，
做的第一件事不是休息，
是擔心：
「我這樣別人會怎麼看？」
「我會不會讓誰失望？」
「我請假他們會不會覺得我不行？」

你用最後一點能量
去顧慮那些根本沒有要幫你的人，
結果什麼都沒解決，
只是把自己消耗得更乾。

殘酷的事實是：
當你倒下，
沒人會記得你撐了多久，
他們只會記得你最後做不到。

所以「先照顧你自己」
不是自私，
是基本的維修。

你不是一個無限續杯的水壺，
你會見底。
而且見底之後，
沒有人會自動幫你裝水。

這句台詞真正在說：
今天的你如果不撐住，
明天的你連選擇的權利都沒有。

先活下來，
再來處理別人怎麼看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明已經很累、卻還在擔心「別人會怎麼想」的朋友——這句他需要看一百遍
variations: 進階版：今天做一件你「以為別人會介意，但其實沒人會在意」的事——早走十五分鐘下班、推掉一個不重要的飯局、把已讀放著兩小時——你會發現天沒有塌

en.content:
There's a line from Doctor Slump
that people keep screenshotting:
"Stop worrying so much about how others see you.
Take care of yourself first.
If today-you is okay,
today-you can help tomorrow-you."

Sounds like a comfort line.
It's actually a very hard reminder.

Most people, in a low point,
don't rest first.
They worry first:
'How will this look?'
'Am I letting someone down?'
'If I take time off, will they think I can't handle it?'

You spend your last drop of energy
worrying about people who weren't going to help you anyway,
and nothing gets solved.
You just drain faster.

The brutal truth:
when you collapse,
no one remembers how long you held on.
They only remember the moment you couldn't.

So 'take care of yourself first'
isn't selfishness.
It's basic maintenance.

You're not a bottomless cup.
You run dry.
And when you do,
no one shows up to refill you.

What the line really means:
if today-you doesn't make it,
tomorrow-you doesn't get to choose anything.

Survive today first.
Deal with their opinions later.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's already exhausted but still worrying 'what will people think' — they need to read this a hundred times
variations: Advanced: do one thing today you assumed 'people would mind' but no one actually will — leave fifteen minutes early, decline an unimportant dinner, let a message sit on read for two hours — the sky will not fall

items.sourceNote: Beauty321 韓劇經典名場面，《鬼怪》九百年告白橋段延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《鬼怪》裡那段被反覆截圖的告白：
「跟你在一起的每一天都很耀眼。
天氣好的日子、
天氣不好的日子、
天氣剛剛好的日子，
每一天，都很美好。」

聽起來像情話，
其實是一個很高難度的承諾。

喜歡一個人「在好天氣的時候」很容易，
他光鮮、有趣、心情好，
你怎麼看都閃閃發光。

難的是壞天氣那天——
他煩躁、沒耐性、一句話都不想說，
你還能說：
「今天也很美好。」

更難的是不上不下的那一天——
沒有吵架、也沒有浪漫，
只是一起吃晚餐、各自滑手機、十一點睡覺。

大部分關係，
都是輸在這種「沒事的日子」。

所以這句台詞真正在說的是：
如果你能在他不發光的時候
還願意說「今天也很好」，
那才算是真的喜歡。

九百年等的不是煙火，
是有人願意陪你過一個普通的星期三。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在跟伴侶經歷「平淡期」、開始懷疑感情的朋友——告訴他平淡才是答案，不是問題
variations: 進階版：今晚問自己一個問題——「如果他今天什麼都沒做、什麼都沒說，我還想跟他在同一個空間嗎？」答案是真的，不是浪漫

en.content:
That endlessly screenshotted line from Goblin:
"Every day I spent with you shined.
The days the weather was good,
the days it wasn't,
the days it was just right —
every single day was beautiful."

Sounds like a love line.
It's actually a brutally high bar.

Loving someone on the good-weather days is easy.
They're charming, funny, in a great mood,
they glow no matter what angle you look.

The hard part is the bad-weather day —
they're irritable, short-tempered, monosyllabic,
and you still get to say:
"Today was good too."

The even harder part: the in-between day.
No fight, no romance.
Just dinner, separate phones, asleep by eleven.

Most relationships don't lose to the storms.
They lose to the Wednesdays.

So what the line really means:
if you can still say 'today was beautiful'
on a day he wasn't shining,
that's the real thing.

Nine hundred years wasn't a wait for fireworks.
It was a wait for someone willing to spend
an ordinary Wednesday with you.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend in a 'quiet phase' with their partner who's started questioning the relationship — the flatness is the answer, not the problem
variations: Advanced: ask yourself tonight — 'if they did nothing, said nothing today, would I still want to be in the same room?' That answer is real. Romance isn't

items.sourceNote: UDN 女子漾《愛情怎麼翻譯？》金宣虎告白金句延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《愛情怎麼翻譯？》裡有一句被瘋傳的告白：
「讀懂彼此的語言，
心意相通的那一刻，
就是兩顆心走進彼此的開始。」

聽起來很文藝，
其實是一個關係的核心測試。

大部分感情失敗的原因
不是不夠喜歡，
是不夠懂。

他說「我沒事」的時候，
你聽到的是「我沒事」，
還是「我不想再解釋一次」？

他傳「先睡了」的時候，
你看到的是字面意思，
還是「今天太累、不想吵」？

會講同一種語言的人很多，
會翻譯彼此那套私人語言的，
通常一輩子遇不到幾個。

所以這句台詞真正在說的是：
愛情的開始
不是心動的那一秒，
是你發現他不用解釋
你就懂的那一秒。

如果你跟一個人講話
永遠要附上一份「使用說明書」，
那不是緣分還沒到，
那是頻率根本不對。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總在抱怨「他怎麼都不懂我」的朋友——也許不是他不懂，是你們的語言本來就不同
variations: 進階版：今天觀察一下——你和他講話的時候，需不需要『多解釋一遍』？需要的次數越多，翻譯成本就越高，這就是答案

en.content:
From Love is for Suckers (Korean title), a line that's been everywhere:
"Learning each other's language,
the moment your hearts speak the same one —
that's when two people actually begin."

Sounds poetic.
It's actually a relationship stress test.

Most relationships don't fail because people stop caring.
They fail because no one's translating anymore.

When they say 'I'm fine,'
do you hear 'I'm fine'
or 'I don't want to explain again'?

When they text 'going to bed,'
do you read the words
or the exhaustion behind them?

Lots of people speak your language.
Very few learn the private dialect
you only use with them.

So what the line really means:
love doesn't start at the heart-flutter moment.
It starts the moment you realize
you don't have to explain — they already got it.

If every conversation needs a user manual,
it's not bad timing.
The frequencies are just wrong.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps complaining 'they just don't get me' — maybe it's not them. Maybe the languages don't match
variations: Advanced: today, count how often you have to 'explain it one more time' to them. The higher the count, the higher the translation cost. That's your answer

items.sourceNote: Pinkvilla《我的出走日記》金智媛經典獨白延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《我的出走日記》裡有一句被收藏成桌布的台詞：
「我想被解放。
從某個我不知道是什麼
但一直壓著我的東西裡，
解放出來。」

聽起來像在抱怨，
其實是一個非常準確的診斷。

大部分人並不是不快樂，
他們是「被一種說不出來的東西
慢慢壓著」。

那個東西可能是：
父母在你二十歲時種下的期待、
同齡人在你三十歲時擺出的成績、
社群媒體在你今天滑開時推送的「別人的生活」。

你說不出是哪一件事讓你累，
但起床、上班、回家、睡覺，
你一直在一個你沒有同意過的時刻表裡跑。

「想被解放」
不是想離職、不是想分手、不是想消失，
是想要——
做一件「沒有人會打分數」的事，
哪怕只有十分鐘。

所以這句台詞真正在問你的是：
你今天，有沒有一個瞬間
是「沒有在被誰看著」的？

如果沒有，
那不是你不努力，
那是你還沒有把自己拿回來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明什麼都好、卻一直覺得「不對勁」的朋友——這句他需要框起來放桌上
variations: 進階版：今天找十分鐘，做一件『沒有目的、沒有產出、沒人會問結果』的事——發呆、看雲、吃一塊不該吃的蛋糕——這十分鐘叫你的

en.content:
From My Liberation Notes, a line people save as their wallpaper:
"I want to be liberated.
From something I can't name,
but has been pressing on me all this time.
I want out."

Sounds like a complaint.
It's actually a very accurate diagnosis.

Most people aren't unhappy.
They're being slowly compressed by
something they can't quite name.

That something could be:
the expectations your parents planted at twenty,
the scoreboard your peers are flashing at thirty,
the 'someone else's life' your feed serves you today.

You can't point to a single thing that's tiring you,
but wake up, work, home, sleep —
you've been running a schedule you never signed.

'I want to be liberated'
doesn't mean quit, break up, disappear.
It means:
do one thing today
that no one is grading,
even if it's just ten minutes.

So what the line is really asking:
did you have a single moment today
that nobody was watching?

If not,
it's not that you didn't try hard enough.
It's that you haven't taken yourself back yet.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose life looks 'fine' on paper but keeps feeling 'off' — this is the line they need framed on their desk
variations: Advanced: find ten minutes today for something with no goal, no output, no one asking how it went — stare at the ceiling, watch clouds, eat the cake you 'shouldn't' — those ten minutes are yours

items.sourceNote: POPBEE《淚之女王》金秀賢經典告白整理延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《淚之女王》裡有一句讓很多結了婚的人沉默的話：
「就算可以重新選一次，
我還是會選你。
不是因為你最好，
是因為你是我選過的那一個。」

聽起來像情話，
其實是一個成熟到很現實的承諾。

年輕的時候，
大家都想要「最對的人」，
好像愛情是一道選擇題，
選對了就一輩子順風順水。

婚姻不是選擇題，
是申論題。
你選的那個人，
後來會跟你一起變成另外兩個人——
更胖一點、更累一點、更會嘆氣一點。

那個「最對的人」根本不存在，
只有「你決定要繼續走下去的那一個」。

所以這句台詞真正在說的是：
愛情不是一直在找答案，
是在你已經選了之後，
一次又一次地告訴自己——
「我選的這個，
我願意繼續為他做選擇。」

這比心動難多了，
但這才是把日子過下去的東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個結婚幾年、開始懷疑「當初是不是選錯」的朋友——選擇從來不是一次性的，每天都在重選
variations: 進階版：今天問自己——『如果今天認識他，我還會選他嗎？』如果答案是會，恭喜你；如果答案需要想三秒，那不是他變了，是該坐下來談談了

en.content:
From Queen of Tears, a line that quiets every married person in the room:
"Even if I could choose again,
I'd still choose you.
Not because you're the best one,
but because you're the one I chose."

Sounds like a love line.
It's actually one of the most adult promises in the language.

When you're young,
you want the 'right' person,
as if love is a multiple-choice question
and picking correctly buys you a smooth life.

Marriage isn't multiple choice.
It's an essay question.
The person you picked
becomes two new people next to you —
slightly heavier, slightly more tired, slightly more sighing.

There is no 'most right person.'
There is only 'the one you keep deciding to walk with.'

So what the line really means:
love isn't a search for the answer.
Love is, after you've already chosen,
telling yourself one more time —
'this one. I'll keep choosing this one.'

That's harder than butterflies.
And that's what actually keeps the days going.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's been married a few years and started wondering 'did I pick wrong' — the choice was never one-time. It's daily
variations: Advanced: ask yourself today — 'if I met them today, would I still pick them?' If the answer is yes, good. If it takes three seconds, they didn't change. You two just need to sit down and talk

items.sourceNote: POPBEE低潮經典韓劇台詞整理，《鬼怪》表白場景延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《鬼怪》裡那句被截圖一萬次的告白：
「和妳在一起的時光全都很耀眼。
因為天氣好，
因為天氣不好，
因為天氣剛剛好。」

年輕的時候你以為這是情話，
長大之後你才看懂，
這是一種非常難達到的狀態。

大部分的關係是這樣的：
天氣好的時候，他在；
天氣不好的時候，他在抱怨；
天氣剛剛好的時候，他在滑手機。

會讓「所有天氣都耀眼」的人，
不是因為他特別會說話，
是因為他在的時候，
你不需要看天氣決定今天的心情。

所以這句台詞真正在篩的是：
你身邊那個人，
是讓你心情跟著天氣走的人，
還是讓天氣跟著你心情走的人？

第一種叫戀愛，
第二種叫家。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在分不清「心動」和「安心」差別的朋友——心動會過去，安心才會留下
variations: 進階版：今天下雨，你第一個想到的人，不是最帥的那個，是『會記得提醒你帶傘』的那個——那才是答案

en.content:
From Goblin, the confession that's been screenshotted a million times:
"Every moment with you was bright.
Because the weather was good.
Because the weather was bad.
Because the weather was just right."

When you're young, it sounds like a love line.
When you're older, you realize
it's actually a very hard state to reach.

Most relationships go:
good weather, they're around.
Bad weather, they're complaining.
Perfect weather, they're on their phone.

The person who makes 'every weather bright'
isn't smooth-talking.
They're just the kind of person whose presence
means you don't check the forecast
before deciding how you'll feel today.

So what this line is actually filtering for:
is the person next to you
the one whose mood moves with the weather,
or the one who makes the weather move with yours?

The first one is dating.
The second one is home.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who still can't tell 'butterflies' apart from 'peace' — butterflies leave. Peace stays
variations: Advanced: it's raining today. The first person you think of isn't the best-looking one. It's the one who'd text 'bring an umbrella.' That's your answer

items.sourceNote: Cosmo《愛的迫降》經典台詞整理，李正赫獨白延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《愛的迫降》裡有一句讓人重看十次的台詞：
「有時候，
搭錯的那班車，
反而會把你帶到對的車站。」

聽起來像安慰，
其實是一種非常成熟的人生觀。

我們從小被訓練成「不要犯錯」，
所以每一個選錯科系、進錯公司、
愛錯人的瞬間，
都會被自己解讀成「人生軌道偏了」。

但人生不是地鐵，
沒有規定哪一站是「對的」。

你以為你坐錯車，
其實你只是去到了一個
本來不會出現在你計劃裡的地方——
那個地方有你沒想過會遇到的人，
沒想過會說的話，
沒想過會變成的自己。

所以這句台詞真正在說的是：
別再追究「當初要是不那樣選就好了」，
因為你現在站的這一站，
是那一次「錯」帶你來的。

而你現在站在這裡，
才能讀到這句話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在後悔某個選擇的朋友——他現在認識的所有好的人事物，都是那個「錯」換來的
variations: 進階版：今天回頭看你以為的『最大的錯』，數一下從那之後遇到的好東西——通常會發現，沒有那個錯，這些都不會出現

en.content:
From Crash Landing on You, a line worth a tenth rewatch:
"Sometimes the wrong train
takes you to the right station."

Sounds like a consolation.
It's actually a very grown-up worldview.

We're trained from childhood not to mess up,
so every wrong major, wrong job, wrong person
feels like the rails of our life got bent.

But life isn't the subway.
Nobody put a sign on the 'correct' stop.

You thought you took the wrong train.
Really, you just ended up somewhere
that was never in your itinerary —
with people you wouldn't have met,
conversations you wouldn't have had,
a version of yourself you wouldn't have become.

So what the line is really saying:
stop relitigating 'if only I hadn't.'
The stop you're standing at right now
was reached by that 'wrong' choice.

And you're standing here
because of it,
reading this.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still beating themselves up over a choice — every good thing in their life now showed up because of that 'mistake'
variations: Advanced: look back at the 'biggest mistake' you think you made, then count the good things that arrived after it. Usually, without the mistake, none of them show up

items.sourceNote: Glossika《我的大叔》經典台詞分析，朴東勳獨白延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《我的大叔》裡有一句被觀眾哭著抄下來的話：
「沒什麼大不了的。
人生本來就沒什麼。」

第一次看，覺得很喪。
第二次看，覺得很真。
第三次看，才發現這是一種——
非常溫柔的解脫。

我們從小被教「人生很重要」、
「每一個選擇都會影響你一輩子」、
「不能輸在起跑點」。

所以一旦哪一步走錯，
一份工作沒做好、一段感情沒走完、
一次考試沒考好，
我們就在心裡判自己「人生完了」。

但這句台詞在說的是：
人生本來就不是一條
「不能出錯」的軌道，
它本來就是一連串
「沒什麼大不了」的事情組合起來的。

你今天遇到的那件
讓你睡不著的事，
五年以後回頭看，
大概率是「沒什麼」。

不是這件事不重要，
是你會變得比這件事大。

所以這句話的真正用法是：
當你今天覺得快撐不住的時候，
大聲跟自己說一次——
「沒什麼大不了，人生本來就沒什麼。」

說完以後，
你會發現你還能再走一步。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個今天把一件小事放大成世界末日的朋友——這句不是叫他放棄，是叫他放下肩膀
variations: 進階版：今天遇到的事，先用三十年後的自己看一次——大部分時候，那個你會笑出來

en.content:
From My Mister, a line viewers copied down through tears:
"It's nothing.
Life is nothing, anyway."

First time you hear it, it sounds bleak.
Second time, it sounds honest.
Third time, you finally see it for what it is —
a very gentle release.

We grew up being told life is important.
Every choice will follow you forever.
Don't lose at the starting line.

So the moment one step goes wrong —
a job we fumbled, a relationship that didn't last,
a test we bombed —
we quietly sentence ourselves to 'life is over.'

But what this line is actually saying:
life was never a track
where you weren't allowed to mess up.
It was always a string
of 'not that big a deal' things
added together.

That thing keeping you up tonight —
five years from now,
the odds are high it's 'nothing.'

Not because it doesn't matter.
Because you'll have grown bigger than it.

So the actual way to use this line:
when tonight feels like too much,
say it out loud once —
"It's nothing. Life is nothing, anyway."

And somehow,
you find one more step in you.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's turned today's small thing into the end of the world — it's not telling them to give up. It's telling them to drop their shoulders
variations: Advanced: look at today's problem through the eyes of you in thirty years. Most of the time, that version of you is laughing

items.sourceNote: Vogue《低谷醫生》治癒金句整理，余廷宇獨白延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《低谷醫生》裡有一句被很多人存進手機備忘錄的話：
「就算今天很幸福，
明天也可能會忽然變得悲傷。
但是沒關係，
有悲傷的我，
就會有幸福的我。」

大部分人對「幸福」有一個很大的誤會：
以為幸福是一種「狀態」，
進去以後就應該一直待著，
出來就是「失敗」。

所以一旦今天心情變差，
我們就懷疑——
「是不是我哪裡做錯了？
是不是我又回到原點？
是不是我根本不配快樂？」

但這句台詞在說的是：
幸福不是一棟你要搬進去的房子，
它是天氣。
會晴，會雨，會有颱風，
會有那種你一覺醒來覺得「世界很好」的早晨，
也會有那種你連起床都困難的下午。

而你之所以能感覺到「幸福」，
正是因為你也認得「悲傷」。
沒有低點的人，
感受不到高點。

所以這句話真正在告訴你：
你今天的低潮，
不是你壞掉了，
是你的感受系統還在運作。

別把每一次情緒下滑
當成「人生倒退」，
那只是天氣換了一下。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個今天無緣無故心情很差、然後又開始怪自己「為什麼又這樣」的朋友——情緒沒有錯，怪自己才是
variations: 進階版：今天心情不好的時候，不要問『為什麼』，問『現在需要什麼』——一杯熱的、一通電話、一場澡——通常答案比理由有用

en.content:
From Doctor Slump, a line lots of people save to their notes app:
"Even if today is happy,
tomorrow might suddenly turn sad.
But that's okay.
As long as there's a sad me,
there will also be a happy me."

Most people have one big misunderstanding about happiness:
they think it's a 'state' —
once you're in, you should stay in,
and leaving means you failed.

So the moment today feels worse,
we start interrogating ourselves —
"Did I do something wrong?
Am I back at square one?
Do I just not deserve to feel good?"

What this line is actually saying:
happiness isn't a house you move into.
It's weather.
There's sun, rain, occasional storms,
mornings where the world feels generous,
afternoons where getting out of bed is the whole day.

The reason you can feel 'happy' at all
is because you also recognize 'sad.'
People with no lows
don't get to feel highs.

So what the line is really telling you:
today's dip
isn't proof you're broken.
It's proof your feelings system is working.

Stop reading every mood drop
as 'my life is regressing.'
The weather just changed.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose mood tanked today for no reason and is now blaming themselves for 'doing it again' — the feeling isn't wrong. Blaming yourself is
variations: Advanced: when today feels bad, stop asking 'why.' Ask 'what do I need right now' — a hot drink, a phone call, a shower. The answer is usually more useful than the reason

items.sourceNote: UDN女子漾《苦盡柑來遇見你》21則金句整理，愛純鋼斧獨白延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《苦盡柑來遇見你》裡有一句被截圖到爛的台詞：
「我們可能會有餓肚子的時候，
但你永遠不會傷我的心。
就算有人給我金斧、銀斧，
我只要鋼斧，
鋼斧才有用。」

年輕的時候你會覺得這只是浪漫，
年紀越大才看懂——
這是一個女生用一輩子的時間
做出來的成本計算。

金斧很閃，銀斧很美，
但兩個都是「拿來看的」。
會陪你下田、修屋頂、
在颱風天把窗戶釘好的，
是那一把又重又黑的鋼斧。

大部分人選錯的地方在於——
他們看條件，沒看用途。
看誰家境好、誰學歷高、誰滑手機的姿勢比較帥，
卻沒看「他願不願意在你發燒的凌晨三點
下樓買藥」。

金斧銀斧不會錯，
錯的是你以為那就是人生的工具。

所以這句台詞真正在問的是：
你身邊那個人，
是拿來給別人看的，
還是真的拿來一起過日子的？

會傷你心的鋼斧，
還不如沒有鋼斧。
但找到一把不會傷你的——
你就可以餓肚子也甘願。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在「他條件很好」和「他對我很好」之間糾結的朋友——鋼斧的重點從來不在金屬
variations: 進階版：今天列兩欄——『我跟別人說起他時用的形容詞』、『我半夜需要他時他做的事』。哪一欄比較長，答案就在那邊

en.content:
From When Life Gives You Tangerines, the line that's been screenshotted to death:
"We might go hungry sometimes,
but you'll never break my heart.
Even if someone offered me a gold axe or a silver axe,
I just want the steel one.
The steel one is the only one that works."

When you're young it sounds like romance.
When you're older you finally see it —
it's a cost calculation
a woman spent her whole life building.

Gold axes shine. Silver axes look nice.
Both are for display.
The one that's going to work the field with you,
fix the roof, nail the windows shut before the typhoon,
is the heavy, blackened steel one.

Where most people pick wrong:
they shop for specs, not for purpose.
Whose family has money, whose degree is shinier,
whose phone-scrolling posture looks cooler —
but not 'will he go downstairs at 3 AM
to buy you medicine when you're burning up.'

Gold and silver aren't the problem.
The problem is mistaking them for life tools.

So what this line is really asking:
is the person next to you
for showing off,
or for actually living a life with?

A steel axe that hurts you
is worse than no axe at all.
But find one that won't —
and you'll be fine going hungry sometimes.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend stuck between 'he looks good on paper' and 'he treats me well' — the point of the steel axe was never the metal
variations: Advanced: make two columns today — 'adjectives I use when describing him to others' vs. 'things he actually did at 2 AM when I needed him.' Whichever column is longer is your answer

items.sourceNote: Marie Claire《苦盡柑來遇見你》100金句整理，「不要躺著動也不動」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《苦盡柑來遇見你》裡有一句被觀眾抄在筆記本上的話：
「要是哪天你覺得日子辛苦到撐不下去了，
不要躺著動也不動，
要起來跟人生拼到底。」

第一眼看像熱血雞湯，
再看一遍，才發現這句話的重點
不在「拼到底」，
在「不要躺著動也不動」。

大部分人的低潮不是哭出來的，
是「不動」出來的。
不洗澡、不回訊息、不開窗簾、
不知道今天是幾號、
外送箱在門口疊成一座小山。

那種狀態不可怕，
可怕的是它有一種詭異的舒適感——
你會跟自己說「我只是累」，
然後一週、兩週就這樣過去了。

這句台詞真正在說的是：
你不需要立刻變強，
不需要立刻好起來，
不需要立刻有人生方向。

你只需要——
站起來，
去洗一個澡，
或者把窗簾拉開十公分。

那十公分的陽光，
才是「跟人生拼到底」的第一步。

撐不下去的時候，
不要躺著等好起來，
好起來不會自己來敲門。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個最近窗簾拉了三天、訊息沒回兩天的朋友——這句不是叫他振作，是叫他先站起來
variations: 進階版：今天低潮的時候，給自己一個『最小動作清單』——洗臉、開窗、走出房間三公尺。完成一個就算贏，不需要拼到底，先拼到下一個動作

en.content:
From When Life Gives You Tangerines, a line viewers copied into their notebooks:
"One day life might get so hard you feel like you can't go on.
Don't just lie there motionless.
Get up and fight life to the end."

First read it sounds like a pep talk.
Read it again and the real weight
is not in 'fight to the end' —
it's in 'don't just lie there motionless.'

Most low points aren't loud.
They're 'not moving.'
Not showering. Not replying.
Not opening the curtains.
Not knowing what day it is.
Delivery boxes stacking into a small mountain by the door.

That state isn't scary on its own.
What's scary is how oddly comfortable it gets —
you tell yourself 'I'm just tired,'
and then one week, two weeks, gone.

What this line is really saying:
you don't have to suddenly be strong,
you don't have to suddenly be okay,
you don't have to suddenly know what you're doing with your life.

You just have to —
get up,
take a shower,
or open the curtains four inches.

Those four inches of sunlight
are what 'fighting life to the end' actually starts as.

When you feel like you can't,
don't lie there waiting to feel better.
Feeling better never comes knocking on its own.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose curtains have been closed for three days and whose texts have been unread for two — this isn't telling them to fight. It's telling them to stand up first
variations: Advanced: on a bad day, write a 'smallest possible action list' — wash face, open window, walk three meters out of the room. Finishing one counts as winning. Don't fight to the end. Fight to the next action

items.sourceNote: UDN女子漾《精神病房也會迎來清晨》35句扎心金句，多恩護士獨白延伸改寫
tags: 精神病房也會迎來清晨

zh-tw.content:
《精神病房也會迎來清晨》裡有一句讓人忍不住寫下來的話：
「任何人都會生病。
天亮前的那一刻，總是最漆黑。
但有件事是肯定的——
沒有人一開始就是病人，
也沒有人到最後還是病人。
怎麼可能永遠都是黑夜呢？
清晨很快就會來。」

大部分人在低潮裡會犯一個錯：
以為「現在的自己」=「以後的自己」。

今天焦慮，就覺得「我這輩子都是焦慮的人」。
今天憂鬱，就覺得「我永遠走不出來」。
今天什麼都做不了，就覺得「我已經壞掉了」。

但這句台詞在說的是：
「狀態」不是「身份」。
你今天生病，
不代表你是一個「病人」。
你今天撐不住，
不代表你是一個「撐不住的人」。

夜晚最深的時候，
不是世界要結束，
是黎明在準備了。

所以這句話真正在告訴你：
如果你今天覺得很黑，
不要急著解釋「為什麼」，
也不要急著證明「我沒事」，
更不要急著定義「我是不是壞掉了」。

你只需要在黑裡——
再多待一會兒。

清晨會來，
它從來沒有失約過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個今天覺得「永遠走不出來」的朋友——黑夜不是世界的版本，是它的一個時段
variations: 進階版：低潮的時候，把『我永遠』改成『我今天』——『我永遠這樣』→『我今天這樣』，光是換這兩個字，肩膀就會輕一半

en.content:
From Daily Dose of Sunshine, a line you can't help writing down:
"Anyone can get sick.
The moment before dawn is always the darkest.
But one thing is certain —
no one starts out as a patient,
and no one stays a patient forever.
How could it possibly always be night?
Morning is coming soon."

Most people make one mistake during a low point:
they assume 'who I am right now' = 'who I'll be forever.'

Anxious today, so 'I'm an anxious person for life.'
Depressed today, so 'I'll never get out of this.'
Unable to function today, so 'I'm broken now.'

What this line is saying:
a state is not an identity.
Being sick today
doesn't make you 'a sick person.'
Not coping today
doesn't make you 'someone who can't cope.'

The deepest part of the night
isn't the world ending.
It's morning getting ready.

So what the line is really telling you:
if today feels dark,
don't rush to explain 'why,'
don't rush to prove 'I'm fine,'
don't rush to decide 'am I broken now.'

Just stay in the dark
a little longer.

Morning is coming.
It has never once failed to show up.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who feels today like they'll 'never get out' — the night isn't a version of the world. It's one of its hours
variations: Advanced: in a low moment, swap 'I'm always' for 'I am today' — 'I'm always like this' → 'I am like this today.' Just changing those two words takes half the weight off your shoulders

items.sourceNote: 多多看電影《機智醫生生活2》50句經典台詞整理，安政源告白延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《機智醫生生活》裡有一句被很多人剪進婚禮影片的台詞：
「我跟妳交往，
不是為了要跟妳結婚，
是想跟妳一起生活一輩子。」

聽起來只是換個說法，
其實是兩種完全不同的愛。

「想跟妳結婚」是一個目標——
戒指、登記、婚宴、合照，
做完就達標了，
然後呢？沒有了。

「想跟妳一起生活一輩子」是一種日常——
誰倒垃圾、誰煮晚餐、
誰半夜三點抱著貓去獸醫院、
誰在你不講話的時候
安靜地遞一杯水過來。

大部分婚姻過得苦，
不是因為兩個人不愛了，
是因為他們以為「結婚」是答案，
然後發現結完才是題目開始。

所以這句台詞真正在篩的是：
你身邊那個人，
是把你當「目標」的人，
還是把你當「日常」的人？

目標會被達成、被遺忘、被換成下一個。
日常會被一起吃、一起睡、一起變老。

會說「想跟妳一起生活一輩子」的人，
通常不會說很多情話。
但他會在你忘記吃藥的那天，
把藥盒放在你看得到的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在等對方「求婚」的朋友——求婚是事件，一起生活是答案
variations: 進階版：今天回想——『他最近一次默默為你做的事是什麼』？如果想得出來，恭喜你；如果想不出來，那個人在跟你戀愛，不是在跟你生活

en.content:
From Hospital Playlist, a line people clip into their wedding videos:
"I'm not dating you
because I want to marry you.
I'm dating you because I want to live a whole life with you."

Sounds like a rephrasing.
It's actually two completely different kinds of love.

'I want to marry you' is a goal —
the ring, the paperwork, the reception, the photos.
Finish them, target hit.
And then? Nothing.

'I want to live a whole life with you' is a routine —
who takes out the trash, who cooks dinner,
who carries the cat to the vet at 3 AM,
who quietly hands you a glass of water
when you've gone quiet.

Most marriages get heavy
not because love is gone,
but because both people thought 'getting married' was the answer,
then found out the questions only started after.

So what this line is really filtering for:
is the person next to you
treating you like a goal,
or like a daily routine?

Goals get achieved, forgotten, replaced with the next one.
Routines get eaten with you, slept beside, grown old together.

The person who says 'I want to live a life with you'
usually doesn't say many love lines.
But on the day you forget your medicine,
they'll put the pill box where you'll see it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still waiting for the 'proposal' — the proposal is an event. Living together is the answer
variations: Advanced: think about today — what was the last quiet thing they did for you without being asked? If you can name it, good. If you can't, that person is dating you, not living with you

items.sourceNote: 《鬼怪》金銀淑經典台詞「跟你在一起的時光全都很耀眼」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《鬼怪》裡有一句被剪進無數MV的台詞：
「跟你在一起的時光全都很耀眼。
因為天氣好，
因為天氣不好，
因為天氣剛剛好——
每一天，都很美好。」

第一次看的時候會覺得很浪漫。
年紀大一點再看，會發現它其實在講另一件事。

不耀眼的不是日子，
是你當時的心情。

同樣是下雨，
跟錯的人在一起，你只看到濕掉的鞋；
跟對的人在一起，你會記得他撐傘時微微歪向你那一邊。

同樣是吃飯，
跟錯的人在一起，你在算什麼時候可以走；
跟對的人在一起，你會希望這頓飯永遠不要結束。

所以這句台詞不是在說「天氣」，
是在說：
「對的人會把所有原本平凡的事，變成你以後會想念的事。」

你以後不會記得那天吃了什麼，
但你會記得他坐在你對面笑出來的樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在懷念某個人的朋友——你懷念的不是他，是你跟他在一起時對世界的眼光
variations: 進階版：分辨對的人有一個極簡測試——跟他做最普通的事，你也覺得不普通；做最普通的事就會覺得很普通的，可能還不是那個人

en.content:
From Goblin, a line clipped into countless edits:
"The time I spent with you was all dazzling.
Because the weather was good,
because the weather was bad,
because the weather was just right —
every single day was beautiful."

First watch: this is romantic.
Watch it again a few years later: it's actually saying something else.

The days weren't the dazzling part.
Your mood was.

Same rain.
With the wrong person, you only notice your wet shoes.
With the right person, you remember how they tilted the umbrella slightly toward you.

Same dinner.
With the wrong person, you're calculating when you can leave.
With the right person, you don't want the meal to end.

So the line isn't really about weather.
It's saying:
"The right person turns all the ordinary things
into things you'll miss later."

You won't remember what you ate that day.
You'll remember how they laughed across the table.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps remembering someone — what you miss isn't them. It's how the world looked when you were with them
variations: Advanced: there's a one-line test for whether someone is right — doing the most ordinary thing with them still doesn't feel ordinary. If ordinary stuff still feels ordinary, it might not be them yet

items.sourceNote: 《黑暗榮耀》金銀淑復仇主題台詞延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《黑暗榮耀》裡文同珢說過一句很冷的話：
「我不會原諒你，
我也不會為你祈禱。」

很多人覺得這句很狠。
但仔細看，它其實不狠，
它只是把一件很多人不敢說的事情講清楚了。

我們從小被教育：
「原諒別人才能放過自己。」
聽起來很有道理，
問題是這句話有一個前提——
那個人「至少」要先承認他傷過你。

沒有承認、沒有道歉、沒有改變的人，
你「原諒」他，不是放過自己，
是又一次告訴他「你可以這樣對我」。

真正的放過自己，
不是強迫自己原諒，
是允許自己——
「我不原諒，但我不再花力氣恨你。」

不原諒，不是繼續糾纏。
不原諒，是不再假裝沒事。

你可以選擇遠離，
可以選擇不再回應，
可以選擇讓他從你的人生裡消失。
你不必同時還要在心裡幫他寫一張赦免狀。

文同珢沒有忘記，
她只是學會了——
「沒有原諒，也可以好好活。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在被「你要學會原諒才能往前走」綁架的朋友——原諒是禮物，不是義務
variations: 進階版：今天起，把『我還沒原諒他』從『我的問題』改成『他還沒值得』——主詞一換，整個故事的方向就變了

en.content:
From The Glory, a line Moon Dong-eun delivers cold:
"I won't forgive you.
And I won't pray for you either."

Most people hear this as harsh.
Look closer — it isn't harsh.
It's just saying out loud what most people are afraid to say.

We were all raised on the line:
"You have to forgive to free yourself."
Sounds wise.
The issue: that sentence has a hidden requirement —
the other person has to at least admit they hurt you first.

For someone who never admitted, never apologized, never changed,
'forgiving' them isn't freeing yourself.
It's telling them once more: 'you can do this to me.'

Real release
isn't forcing forgiveness.
It's giving yourself permission to say:
"I don't forgive you. And I won't spend energy hating you either."

Not forgiving isn't staying tangled.
Not forgiving is refusing to pretend you're fine.

You can walk away.
You can stop responding.
You can let them vanish from your life.
You don't have to also write them a pardon in your head.

Dong-eun didn't forget.
She just learned:
"You can live well without forgiveness."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still being told 'you have to forgive to move on' — forgiveness is a gift, not an obligation
variations: Advanced: starting today, change 'I haven't forgiven them yet' from 'my problem' to 'they haven't earned it yet' — swap the subject, the whole story changes direction

items.sourceNote: 《海岸村恰恰恰》洪斑長人生哲學台詞延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《海岸村恰恰恰》裡洪斑長講過一段話：
「金錢跟成功不是人生唯一重要的東西。
快樂、自我滿足、愛——
人生不是數學題，
沒有標準答案。
你只是被給了一道題，
怎麼解，是你的事。」

很多人聽完只覺得「嗯，雞湯」。
但他真正在講的是一件很實際的事：
你以為的人生「正確答案」，
是別人寫的考古題。

你以為三十歲一定要存到多少錢，
是別人寫的。
你以為三十五歲一定要結婚生小孩，
是別人寫的。
你以為人生過得「對」就是穩定上班買房升遷，
還是別人寫的。

但問題是——
你的人生沒有人生過。
沒有「過來人」，
