只有「跟你不一樣的人」。

所以下次有人跟你說「你應該」的時候，
你只需要在心裡問一句：
「你怎麼知道這是答案？
你也只是在解你自己的題而已啊。」

洪斑長沒有比較成功，
他只是比較早承認：
「我這道題，我自己寫。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被『你都這把年紀了還沒...』壓得喘不過氣的朋友——別人的進度條，不是你的時間軸
variations: 進階版：列出五個你『覺得應該做』的事，旁邊寫『是誰跟我說應該的』——能寫出名字的，幾乎都不是你自己要的

en.content:
From Hometown Cha-Cha-Cha, Chief Hong drops a whole worldview in one paragraph:
"Money and success aren't the only things that matter in life.
Happiness, self-contentment, love —
life isn't a math problem.
There's no right answer.
You're handed a problem.
How you solve it is up to you."

Most people hear this and think 'okay, motivational.'
What he's actually saying is much more practical:
the 'correct answers' you think life has
were written by other people.

The idea that you need a certain amount saved by thirty —
written by someone else.
The idea that you need to be married with kids by thirty-five —
written by someone else.
The idea that 'doing life right' means stable job, mortgage, promotion —
still written by someone else.

The catch:
no one has lived your life before.
There are no 'people who've been through it.'
There are only 'people who lived a different one.'

So next time someone says 'you should,'
ask quietly inside:
"How do you know that's the answer?
You were only solving your own problem."

Chief Hong isn't more successful.
He just admitted earlier:
"This is my problem. I'll write my own answer."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend being crushed by 'at your age you should already...' — someone else's progress bar isn't your timeline
variations: Advanced: list five things you 'feel you should be doing,' then next to each write 'who told me I should.' If you can name them, almost none of it was yours

items.sourceNote: 《今生也請多指教》「裝出來的」人生台詞延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《今生也請多指教》裡有一句很意外的台詞：
「人到死都不會懂事。
大家都是裝出來的。」

第一次聽會笑出來，
但這句話其實救過很多人。

我們從小被告訴：
「長大就會懂了。」
所以你以為三十歲會比二十歲更知道答案，
四十歲會比三十歲更穩定，
五十歲應該已經想清楚了。

然後你三十歲那天醒來，
發現你還是會因為一個訊息沒回難過半天，
還是會在會議裡假裝聽得懂，
還是會半夜兩點懷疑自己選錯路。

你以為是你出問題，
其實不是。

所有人都一樣。
那個你以為已經「想清楚」的主管，
他昨天也跟太太吵架。
那個你覺得「人生很成功」的朋友，
他也偷偷在Google「我是不是憂鬱症」。

沒有人懂事，
只是有些人比較會裝。

所以這句台詞真正在告訴你：
你不用等到「想清楚」才開始生活。
等不到的。

你只需要——
一邊活，一邊裝，一邊偷偷搞清楚一點點，
就已經是大部分人在做的事了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個覺得「別人都比較成熟」的朋友——別人只是比較不被你看到他的崩潰時刻
variations: 進階版：下次你覺得別人『怎麼都這麼有條理』，請記得——你只看到他PPT做完的那一刻，沒看到他凌晨三點還在改

en.content:
From Doctor Cha (Doctor Cha Jeong-suk), one of the most unexpected lines:
"People don't actually grow up before they die.
Everyone is just faking it."

First reaction: laugh.
This line has actually saved a lot of people.

We were raised on:
"You'll understand when you're older."
So you assumed thirty would know more than twenty,
forty would be steadier than thirty,
fifty would have figured it out.

Then you wake up on your thirtieth birthday
and realize you can still be ruined for an afternoon
by someone not replying.
You still fake understanding in meetings.
You still doubt every choice at 2 AM.

You assume something is wrong with you.
Nothing is.

Everyone is the same.
The manager you assume has it 'figured out'
fought with their spouse last night.
The friend whose life looks 'successful'
is quietly Googling 'am I depressed.'

Nobody has it figured out.
Some people are just better at faking it.

So what the line is really telling you:
you don't have to wait until you 'understand' to start living.
That moment isn't coming.

You just —
live, fake it, secretly figure out a little more,
and that's already what most adults are doing.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who thinks 'everyone else is more mature' — they're just less visible when they fall apart
variations: Advanced: next time you think 'why is everyone else so put together' — remember you only see the moment the deck was finished, not them rewriting it at 3 AM

items.sourceNote: 《請回答1988》家人主題台詞「家人不是用血緣，是用陪伴」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《請回答1988》裡有一句不顯眼但很重的台詞：
「家人不一定是血緣，
有時候，是那個一直留下來的人。」

小時候你以為「家人」這兩個字是固定的——
戶口名簿上有名字的人。
長大以後你會慢慢發現，
不是這樣。

有些人血緣很近，
但你跟他說話像在過海關，
每講一句都要先想「會不會被檢查」。

有些人沒有血緣，
但你半夜被退稿哭著打電話，
他第一句是「妳吃飽沒？」
而不是「為什麼要做這行？」

「家人」這個詞，
本來就不是用出生地決定的，
是用「在你最沒形象的時候
還願意看著你」決定的。

所以《1988》裡那群鄰居比親戚還親，
不是因為劇情需要，
是因為現實本來就常常這樣。

所以如果你今天身邊有那種——
不用解釋、不用裝、不用挑時機才能打電話的人，
請珍惜他。

血緣是給你的，
那個人是你自己選的。
後者比較稀有。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個你不是親人但比親人還重要的朋友——直接告訴他，他可能不知道自己對你有這麼重
variations: 進階版：今天列出三個你『不用裝就能打電話』的人——這份名單比你的存款數字更接近你人生的真實財富

en.content:
From Reply 1988, a line that's quiet but heavy:
"Family isn't always blood.
Sometimes it's the person who stayed."

As a kid, you assume 'family' is fixed —
the names on the household record.
As you get older, you slowly realize:
it isn't.

Some people are blood-close,
but talking to them feels like going through customs.
Every sentence pre-screened: 'will this get flagged?'

Some people share no blood,
but when you cry-call them at midnight after a rejection,
their first line is 'have you eaten?'
not 'why are you even doing this job?'

The word 'family'
was never decided by birthplace.
It's decided by 'who's still willing to look at you
when you're at your least presentable.'

So in Reply 1988, those neighbors feel closer than relatives
not because the plot needed it —
real life often works exactly like that.

If today you have someone
you don't have to explain to, perform for, or time your calls around,
treasure them.

Blood was given to you.
That person you chose.
The second category is the rare one.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who isn't blood but matters more — tell them directly. They may not know how much weight they hold for you
variations: Advanced: today, list three people you can call without performing — that list is closer to your real net worth than the number in your bank account

items.sourceNote: 《孤單又燦爛的神－鬼怪》「跟你在一起的時光，每一天都很耀眼」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《鬼怪》裡有一句被引用過上千次的台詞：
「跟你在一起的時光，每一天都很耀眼。
因為天氣好，因為天氣不好，
因為天氣剛剛好。」

第一次聽會覺得很文青，
第二次聽你會發現他在說一件很現實的事——

當你真的在乎一個人的時候，
「條件」會消失。

你不會說「等天氣好我們再出門」，
你會說「下雨那就一起在家」。
你不會說「等我比較不忙再聯絡你」，
你會說「我正在忙，但你先說」。
你不會說「等我心情好再陪你」，
你會發現他在你身邊的那一刻，心情就好了。

所謂「每一天都很耀眼」，
不是天氣真的天天剛剛好——
是因為人對了，
天氣怎樣都變成藉口，
而不是阻礙。

所以你怎麼判斷對方是不是那個人？
很簡單：
當你在他面前不需要『等條件成熟』，
當下就是條件。

那大概就是了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老是說「等我比較有空再陪你」的對象——讓他看清這句話本身就是答案
variations: 進階版：把你最近三次拒絕對方邀約的理由列出來，再問自己：『如果今天是我最喜歡的人約我，這個理由還成立嗎？』成立的才是真的，不成立的就是藉口

en.content:
From Goblin, a line that's been quoted a thousand times:
"Every day with you was dazzling.
Because the weather was good,
because the weather was bad,
because the weather was just right."

First listen: poetic.
Second listen: he's describing something very practical —

when you actually care about someone,
'conditions' disappear.

You don't say 'let's go out when the weather's nice,'
you say 'it's raining, let's stay in together.'
You don't say 'I'll text when I'm less busy,'
you say 'I'm busy, but go ahead, tell me.'
You don't say 'I'll hang out when I'm in a better mood,'
you notice your mood lifted the second they walked in.

'Every day was dazzling'
isn't about the weather actually cooperating —
it's that when the person is right,
weather becomes an excuse to be together,
not a reason not to.

So how do you know they're the one?
Simple:
when you don't need 'conditions to be ready' around them —
the moment itself is the condition.

That's probably it.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who keeps saying 'I'll see you when I'm less busy' — let this line do the explaining for you
variations: Advanced: list the last three times you declined plans with someone. Now ask: 'if my favorite person had asked instead, would the reason still hold?' The ones that hold are real. The ones that don't were excuses.

items.sourceNote: 《歡迎來到三達里》愛的溫度概念「不是大小，是溫度」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《歡迎來到三達里》裡有一句很容易錯過、但其實很重要的台詞：
「不是每個人都會嫁給自己最熾熱的那段愛。
愛的大小其實差不多，
差的是——溫度。
是那個讓我可以做自己的溫度。」

這句話救過很多三十歲以後的人。

年輕的時候，
你以為「最愛」一定是那種會讓你心跳停三秒、
哭一整晚、整個夏天失眠的那個人。
那種「熱到燙手」的愛。

然後你年紀大一點才發現——
燙手的東西沒辦法拿太久。
你會放下，你會閃躲，
你會在被燙到第二次的時候默默縮手。

真正陪你走完一輩子的，
不是「最熱」的那個人，
而是「溫度剛好」的那個人——

他不會讓你心跳停三秒，
但他會讓你睡得很安穩。
他不會讓你失眠整個夏天，
但他會記得你討厭吃香菜。
他不會讓你寫日記寫滿五本，
但他會在你忘記繳水費的時候提醒你。

「溫度剛好」聽起來很無聊，
但那其實是成熟的人才看得懂的浪漫。

熾熱會燒完，
剛好的溫度可以煮一輩子的飯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在懷念「當年那段轟轟烈烈」的朋友——剛好的溫度不是平淡，是會留下來的那種愛
variations: 進階版：問自己一個問題：『跟現在這個人在一起的時候，我可不可以做我自己？』可以的話，那個溫度就是對的，不需要再去找燙手的

en.content:
From Welcome to Samdal-ri, a line easy to miss but essential:
"Not everyone marries their most passionate love.
The size of the love wasn't different.
The temperature was.
It was the temperature that let me be myself."

This line has rescued a lot of people past thirty.

When you're young,
you assume 'true love' has to be the one that
stops your heart for three seconds,
keeps you crying all night,
kills your sleep for a whole summer.
The 'too hot to hold' kind.

Then you get a little older and realize —
you can't actually hold hot things for long.
You put them down. You flinch.
The second time you burn yourself, you quietly pull your hand back.

The one who walks the whole road with you
isn't the hottest one,
it's the one at the right temperature —

They don't stop your heart for three seconds,
but you sleep deeply next to them.
They don't keep you up all summer,
but they remember you hate cilantro.
They don't fill five journals,
but they remind you when you forgot to pay the water bill.

'Just right' sounds boring,
but it's the kind of romance only mature people can read.

Hot burns out.
The right temperature can cook meals for a lifetime.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still romanticizing 'that one intense relationship' — right temperature isn't dull, it's the love that actually stays
variations: Advanced: ask yourself one question — 'when I'm with this person, can I be myself?' If yes, that temperature is right. You don't need to keep chasing the burn.

items.sourceNote: 《低谷醫生》「不要在意別人的目光，先照顧你自己」治癒台詞延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《低谷醫生》裡朴信惠的那句台詞，
是低潮時最該記住的話：
「不要再去管別人的眼光了。
你先把自己照顧好。
今天的你要先好好的，
明天的你才有人可以幫。」

我們從小被訓練成「先想別人」——
別讓媽媽擔心、
別讓老闆失望、
別讓朋友覺得你很麻煩、
別讓另一半感覺到你很糟。

所以你在最累的那天，
第一件事還是回訊息給別人說「我沒事」。
你在崩潰的那個晚上，
還是先確認別人有沒有不開心。

你以為這叫成熟，
其實這只是把自己排到第八位。

低潮的時候你會發現一件事——
沒有人能幫一個連自己都不照顧的人。
別人想關心你，他得先找到你；
你連自己都不在了，他要關心誰？

所以「先把自己照顧好」
不是自私，
是基本配備。

今天的你要好好的——
才會有明天的你，
才會有「以後想關心你」的那個人，
還找得到你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明很累但還在硬撐、把自己排在最後面的朋友——讓他知道「自顧」不是自私
variations: 進階版：今天就做一件『只為自己』的小事——不為了交代、不為了拍照、不為了別人問起時有故事可說。純粹只為自己。那才叫照顧自己

en.content:
From Doctor Slump, the Park Shin-hye line
you should keep close on bad days:
"Stop worrying about how other people see you.
Take care of yourself first.
Today's you has to be okay,
so tomorrow's you has someone to help."

We're trained from childhood to 'think of others first' —
don't worry mom,
don't disappoint the boss,
don't make your friends feel you're a burden,
don't let your partner sense you're a mess.

So on your worst day,
the first thing you still do is text back 'I'm fine.'
On the night you're falling apart,
you still check whether anyone else is upset.

You call this maturity.
It's actually just putting yourself eighth.

During a low point you realize something —
nobody can help a person who isn't taking care of themselves.
For someone to care, they have to be able to find you.
If you've already disappeared into other people's needs,
who's left to care for?

So 'take care of yourself first'
isn't selfish.
It's the baseline.

Today's you has to be okay —
so tomorrow's you exists,
and the people who want to be there for you later
can still find you.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's clearly exhausted but still 'managing' — let them know self-care isn't selfishness, it's the minimum
variations: Advanced: do one thing today that's purely for you — no Instagram, no story to tell, no productive justification. Just for you. That's what care actually looks like.

items.sourceNote: 《我的解放日誌》「我想被一個人完全推崇」推崇主題延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《我的解放日誌》裡廉美貞說過一句話，
聽完會讓你愣很久：
「我不要被愛、也不要被安慰。
我想要被推崇。
一次就好，被一個人完完整整地推崇。」

第一次聽會覺得「也太貪心」，
但你想清楚之後會發現——
這句話戳中了一個很多人不敢承認的東西。

我們大部分人在感情裡都得到過「愛」、
得到過「關心」、得到過「陪伴」。
但「推崇」很少。

推崇是什麼？
是對方真的覺得「你這個人就是好」，
不是「我可以接受你的缺點」，
不是「我覺得你還不錯所以選你」，
是——
他覺得你光是存在，就已經值得。

你身上那些你自己覺得很可笑的部分，
他覺得很珍貴。
你跟別人講過很多次、都沒人聽進去的夢，
他記得。
你做了一件你以為沒人會在意的小事，
他偷偷感動。

大部分的關係只能給「愛」，
少數的關係能給「理解」，
但「推崇」需要對方真的相信你這個人——
不是因為你做了什麼，
是因為你是你。

所以廉美貞那句話其實在問：
你這輩子，被誰這樣看過？

如果有，就抓緊。
如果沒有，那不是因為你不夠好，
是因為這種人本來就稀有。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總覺得「我已經很好命了」但內心其實很空的朋友——你缺的不是愛，是被完整看見
variations: 進階版：如果你身邊真的有那個『推崇你』的人，今天傳訊息謝謝他——不是為了任何事，是因為他願意這樣看你，本身就是奇蹟

en.content:
From My Liberation Notes, Yeom Mi-jeong says a line
that leaves you staring at the wall:
"I don't want to be loved.
I don't want to be comforted.
I want to be adored.
Just once — fully adored by one person."

First reaction: 'isn't that asking too much?'
Think about it longer and you realize —
she just named something most people won't admit they want.

Most of us have received 'love' in relationships.
We've received 'care.' We've received 'company.'
Being adored — rare.

What does adored mean?
It means the other person genuinely believes
'you, as a person, are good' —
not 'I can tolerate your flaws,'
not 'I picked you because you're acceptable,'
but —
they think your existence alone is already worth it.

The parts of you that you find embarrassing,
they find precious.
The dream you've told a dozen people and no one really heard,
they remember.
A small thing you did assuming no one would notice,
quietly moved them.

Most relationships can give 'love.'
A few can give 'understanding.'
But 'adoration' needs someone who actually believes in you —
not because of what you've done,
because of who you are.

So Mi-jeong's line is really asking:
In your life, who has ever looked at you like that?

If someone has, hold on.
If no one has — it's not because you're not enough.
It's because that kind of person is genuinely rare.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps saying 'I have nothing to complain about' but feels hollow — what you're missing isn't love, it's being fully seen
variations: Advanced: if there's actually someone who adores you, text them today — not for any reason, just to thank them. Someone willing to see you that way is the miracle itself.

items.sourceNote: POPBEE 2026治癒系韓劇台詞特輯，「情緒生病就跟感冒一樣正常」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句話，
第一次聽到會覺得「也太簡單」，
再聽一次才發現自己愣住——
「情緒生病了，跟感冒一樣正常。」

感冒的時候，
你不會罵自己「為什麼這麼脆弱」，
你會去看醫生、會多喝水、會請假休息。
你會接受「身體就是這樣，今天打不了仗」。

可是情緒生病的時候，
你做的第一件事是——
罵自己。
「為什麼別人都可以，我不行？」
「為什麼這麼小的事我會崩潰？」
「我是不是太玻璃心了？」

你逼自己上班、逼自己社交、
逼自己「振作一點」，
好像情緒不是生病，是犯錯。

但其實它跟感冒一樣——
會來，會走，
需要時間，需要照顧，
不需要你的譴責。

下次心情很糟的時候，
試著對自己說：
「我今天感冒了，只是感冒在心裡。」

你會發現，
光是換一個說法，
你就比較願意對自己好一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個情緒很糟、但一直罵自己「太脆弱」的朋友——身體會感冒，心也會，都不是錯
variations: 進階版：你不會跟感冒的朋友說「你振作一點」，那就也不要跟情緒感冒的自己這樣說——對自己用一樣的標準

en.content:
There's a K-drama line that sounds too simple
until you hear it twice and freeze —
"Emotional illness is as normal as catching a cold."

When you have a cold,
you don't yell at yourself for being weak.
You see a doctor, drink water, take a day off.
You accept that the body has its own schedule today.

But when your emotions get sick,
the first thing you do is —
blame yourself.
"Why can everyone else handle this and I can't?"
"Why am I falling apart over something so small?"
"Am I just too fragile?"

You push yourself to work, to socialize,
to 'snap out of it,'
as if feelings aren't an illness but a mistake.

They work like a cold —
they arrive, they pass.
They need time. They need care.
They do not need your criticism.

Next time you feel awful,
try saying to yourself:
"I have a cold today. It just landed in my head instead of my throat."

You'll notice that just by changing the words,
you become a little more willing
to be kind to yourself.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's struggling emotionally but keeps calling themselves weak — bodies catch colds, and so do hearts. Neither is a failure
variations: Advanced: you wouldn't tell a friend with the flu to 'just toughen up' — apply the same rule to yourself when your emotions are sick

items.sourceNote: Soompi 韓劇金句精選，《海岸村恰恰恰》洪斗植「就算撐傘也會淋濕，那就舉起雙手歡迎雨」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《海岸村恰恰恰》洪斗植說過一句話，
聽完那天你會回想很久：
「人生會遇到很多意外。
就算撐了傘，最後還是會被淋濕。
那不如把手舉起來，歡迎那場雨。」

我們花太多力氣在「不想被淋濕」這件事上——
買最好的傘、查最準的天氣、
挑最安全的路線、
避開所有可能會出錯的選項。

結果呢？
還是會在某一天，
被一場你完全沒預料到的雨淋成落湯雞。

那一刻你會發現，
原來人生大部分的痛苦不是來自於下雨，
是來自於「我明明都做了預防」的不甘心。

洪斗植那句話不是要你放棄撐傘，
是說——
當傘失效的那一天，
你還是可以選擇要怎麼面對。

罵天、罵地、罵那把不爭氣的傘，
或者，
把手舉起來，讓雨打在臉上，
承認「啊，今天就是這樣」。

後者不會讓你比較不濕，
但會讓你比較不痛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明已經很努力預防、結果還是被生活淋成落湯雞的朋友——有時候舉手投降不是輸，是省力
variations: 進階版：下次計畫被打亂的時候，先別急著補救——花三十秒承認「這場雨來了」，再做下一步。少了那三十秒，你會多花三小時在生氣

en.content:
From Hometown Cha-Cha-Cha, Hong Du-sik says
something you'll keep coming back to:
"Life throws unexpected things at you.
Even with an umbrella, you end up soaked.
So you might as well raise your hands
and welcome the rain."

We spend so much energy on 'not getting wet' —
the best umbrella, the most accurate forecast,
the safest route,
avoiding every option that could go wrong.

And still?
One day you'll get caught
in a downpour you never saw coming.

What you realize in that moment is
most of life's pain doesn't come from the rain itself.
It comes from the resentment of
'I prepared for this, and it still happened.'

Du-sik isn't telling you to throw out the umbrella.
He's saying —
on the day the umbrella fails,
you still get to choose how to meet it.

Curse the sky, curse the ground, curse the umbrella,
or —
raise your hands, let the rain hit your face,
admit 'okay, today is just like this.'

The second option doesn't make you less wet.
It makes you less hurt.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who planned everything carefully and life still soaked them — sometimes raising your hands isn't losing, it's saving energy
variations: Advanced: next time a plan falls apart, don't rush to fix it. Spend thirty seconds admitting 'the rain is here' first. Skipping those thirty seconds costs you three hours of being angry

items.sourceNote: POPBEE《淚之女王》10句經典台詞特輯，「愛不是甜言蜜語、是一起承受痛苦」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《淚之女王》裡有一句話，
如果你結過婚或交往超過三年，會特別有感：
「愛不是甜言蜜語、不是一直開心。
愛是一起承受痛苦的時候、
你選擇留下，而不是逃走。」

我們從韓劇學到的愛情大部分是——
初遇心動、雨中接吻、機場追人、生日驚喜。
那些「閃光的瞬間」很美，
但它們不是愛情，
它們是愛情的開場白。

真正的愛情長什麼樣？
是你陪他去醫院做檢查，
結果他緊張到對你發脾氣，
你還是沒走。

是她家裡出事的那半年，
整個人都不像她，
你還是每天回家。

是你失業那段時間，
他沒有說「我相信你」這種台詞，
他只是默默把房租付了。

愛情不是甜的時候的選擇，
是苦的時候你還願不願意。

那個願意的人很少，
所以你身邊如果有一個，
別忘了——
他其實已經很厲害了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在低潮時還在你身邊的人——不用說太多，他會知道你說的是什麼
variations: 進階版：下次你想感謝對方的時候，不要說「謝謝你愛我」，說「謝謝你那天沒走」——後者具體很多，他會記得更久

en.content:
From Queen of Tears, there's a line
that hits harder if you've been married or coupled for years:
"Love isn't sweet words and constant happiness.
Love is when you endure the pain together —
when you choose to stay instead of running away."

Most of what K-dramas teach us about love is —
first sparks, rain kisses, airport chases, birthday surprises.
Those 'shining moments' are beautiful,
but they aren't love.
They're the opening credits.

What does real love actually look like?
It's going with him to the hospital,
him getting nervous and snapping at you,
and you still not leaving.

It's the six months her family fell apart,
she didn't feel like herself,
and you still came home every night.

It's the stretch when you were unemployed —
he didn't say 'I believe in you,'
he just quietly covered the rent.

Love isn't what you choose when things are sweet.
It's whether you're still willing when things are bitter.

The person who's still willing is rare.
So if you have one around you,
don't forget —
they're already doing something extraordinary.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who stayed during your worst stretch — you don't need many words, they'll know what you mean
variations: Advanced: next time you want to thank them, don't say 'thank you for loving me' — say 'thank you for not walking out that day.' The second one is specific, and they'll remember it longer

items.sourceNote: 《來自星星的你》都敏俊「命運」台詞經典金句，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
《來自星星的你》都敏俊那句話，
是很多人手機備忘錄裡偷偷存著的：
「如果有必要相遇，就終究會遇到。
如果遇不到，就是沒必要相遇。
地球人把這種事，叫做命運。」

聽起來很浪漫，
但這句話最厲害的地方不是浪漫——
是它幫你放下「為什麼那個人後來沒有出現在我生命裡」。

我們大部分的痛苦，
都來自於「明明差一點就可以了」。
差一個訊息、差一通電話、
差一次主動、差一個時機。

你會一直回去想：
「如果那天我有去那個聚會……」
「如果那次我有開口問他……」
「如果我們早一點認識……」

但都敏俊這句話的意思是——
如果你們真的有那個份，
不會差一個訊息。
會差一個訊息的，
是時間在告訴你：這次不是。

這不是要你放棄相信緣分，
是要你放過自己。

沒留下的人，
不是你哪裡不夠好，
是這趟相遇的目的已經完成了——
他來教你一件事，
教完了，他就會走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在後悔「當時應該主動一點」的朋友——主動沒有用的話，那本來就不是你的人
variations: 進階版：下次又想起那個「如果當時」的人，試著改說「他來教我這件事，我學會了」——換一個說法，整個故事的重量就不一樣

en.content:
From My Love from the Star, Do Min-jun's line
lives quietly in a lot of people's notes app:
"If a meeting is meant to be, it will happen.
If it doesn't happen, it wasn't meant to.
Earthlings call this fate."

It sounds romantic,
but the real power of this line isn't the romance —
it's that it helps you let go of
'why didn't that person stay in my life?'

Most of our pain comes from
'we were so close.'
One message short, one call short,
one move short, one timing off.

You keep replaying it:
"If I had gone to that gathering..."
"If I had asked him that one time..."
"If we'd met earlier..."

But what Min-jun's line is really saying —
if the two of you were meant to,
it wouldn't have come down to one message.
When it does come down to one message,
time is telling you: not this one.

This isn't asking you to stop believing in fate.
It's asking you to stop blaming yourself.

The person who didn't stay
isn't a sign that you weren't enough.
The purpose of that meeting was already complete —
they came to teach you one thing,
and once you'd learned it, they left.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still regretting 'I should have spoken up back then' — if speaking up wouldn't have worked, that was never your person
variations: Advanced: next time the 'what if' person crosses your mind, try saying 'they came to teach me this, and I learned it' instead. Change the phrasing and the weight of the whole story changes

items.sourceNote: PINKVILLA韓劇金句整理，《鬼怪》孔劉經典告白「不是因為信任你，是因為喜歡你」原創改編

zh-tw.content:
《鬼怪》孔劉那句台詞，
表面看起來只是一句告白：
「不是因為相信你，是因為喜歡你。」

但這句話其實藏了一個很現實的觀察——

大部分的人在感情裡，
會先想：
「他會不會騙我？」
「他這次說的是真的嗎？」
「上次他承諾了，結果呢？」

用「相信」當作前提，
是因為怕受傷。
要等對方證明過、累積夠了，
你才敢踏出下一步。

但喜歡不是這樣運作的。
喜歡是——
就算他可能不會留下，
你還是想對他好；
就算他這次可能會傷你，
你還是會在他需要的時候出現；
就算後來沒有結果，
你也不會後悔當時靠近過。

「不是因為相信，是因為喜歡」，
意思是：
我不再用你的表現決定我要不要愛你，
我用我自己的選擇決定。

這才是真正的成熟告白。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直在問「他到底愛不愛我」的朋友——問題從來不是他愛不愛你，是你要不要愛他
variations: 進階版：下次猶豫要不要對一個人付出，先問自己「就算他離開我也不後悔嗎？」——答案是是，你就敢愛了

en.content:
Gong Yoo's line in Goblin
sounds like just another confession:
"It's not because I trust you. It's because I like you."

But there's a very real observation hiding in it —

Most people in relationships start with:
"Is he going to lie to me?"
"Is what he's saying this time actually true?"
"He promised last time, and look how that went."

We use 'trust' as a prerequisite
because we're afraid of getting hurt.
We wait until they've proven it, racked up enough points,
before we'll take the next step.

But liking someone doesn't work that way.
Liking someone is —
even if he might not stay,
you still want to be good to him;
even if he might hurt you this time,
you still show up when he needs you;
even if it doesn't work out,
you won't regret having gotten close.

"Not because I trust you, because I like you" means:
I'm no longer deciding whether to love you based on your behavior.
I'm deciding based on my own choice.

That's what a grown-up confession actually sounds like.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend stuck asking 'does he even love me' — the question was never whether he loves you, it's whether you're going to love him
variations: Advanced: next time you're debating whether to invest in someone, ask yourself 'would I regret this even if they left?' If the answer is no, you're ready

items.sourceNote: 電影迷《愛的迫降》100句經典台詞語錄，「不是消失的夕陽，是停留的星星」原創改編

zh-tw.content:
《愛的迫降》裡有一句話，
看似簡單卻很多人在分手後才懂：
「不要像夕陽一樣消失，
要像星星一樣留下來。」

夕陽很美，
但夕陽是注定要走的。
它的美，建立在「即將消失」這件事上——
你知道它撐不久，
所以你更用力地看。

很多人談戀愛是當夕陽——
激烈、強烈、難以忘記，
但時間到了就走，
讓你連挽留的機會都沒有。

星星不一樣。
星星不耀眼，
你抬頭甚至要找一下，
但它每天都在那裡，
你需要它的時候它就在。

問題是——
年輕的時候我們都想找夕陽，
以為驚天動地才是愛情。
等到後來才發現：
你真正需要的，
是那個你抬頭就看得到的人。

不是最閃的，
是最在的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛結束一段「轟轟烈烈但留不下來」的感情的朋友——下一個請找願意當星星的人
variations: 進階版：判斷對方是夕陽還是星星，看一件事——他在你不需要他的時候，還在不在你身邊

en.content:
There's a line in Crash Landing on You
that looks simple but most people only get it after a breakup:
"Don't disappear like the sunset.
Stay — like the stars."

Sunsets are beautiful,
but sunsets are always leaving.
Their beauty is built on 'about to vanish' —
you know it won't last,
so you stare harder.

A lot of people date like a sunset —
intense, overwhelming, unforgettable,
but when the time's up they're gone,
and you don't even get the chance to ask them to stay.

Stars are different.
Stars don't dazzle.
You even have to look around to find them.
But they're there every night,
and when you need one, it's there.

The thing is —
when we're young we all want sunsets.
We think love has to be earth-shaking.
It's only later we figure out:
what you actually need
is the person you can find just by looking up.

Not the brightest one.
The one that stays.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who just got out of an 'intense but didn't last' relationship — next time, find someone willing to be a star
variations: Advanced: to tell whether someone's a sunset or a star, watch one thing — are they still around when you don't need them?

items.sourceNote: POPBEE低潮經典韓劇台詞，《我的出走日記》「光是維持普通生活就已經耗盡全力」原創改編

zh-tw.content:
《我的出走日記》有一句話，
戳中所有在城市裡撐著的成年人：
「長大後，光是維持普通的生活，
就已經耗盡全力。
承認自己只想做個平凡人，
一點都不丟臉。」

我們從小被告訴——
要努力、要出色、要與眾不同。
結果長大後發現：
光是準時起床、好好吃飯、按時繳房租、
不要在週日晚上崩潰，
就已經是一場每天上演的小型戰役。

你以為的「沒做出什麼」，
其實是：
沒有放棄、
沒有躺平、
沒有讓自己的人生變成新聞標題、
沒有把焦慮傳染給身邊的人。

這些事不會被表揚，
但每一件都很費力。

所以下次你又想說「我這個年紀怎麼一事無成」，
試著改成——
「我這個年紀，
還能維持一個正常運作的生活，
已經很厲害了。」

平凡不是失敗，
平凡是當代最低調的成就。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個最近又開始問自己「我這幾年到底做了什麼」的朋友——光是還在這裡，就已經是答案
variations: 進階版：下次想列「我這年沒做什麼」清單之前，先列「我這年沒崩潰幾次」清單——你會發現自己其實一直都在贏

en.content:
There's a line in My Liberation Notes
that lands hard for every adult holding it together in the city:
"As an adult, just maintaining an ordinary life
takes everything you have.
Admitting you only want to be ordinary
is nothing to be ashamed of."

We grew up being told —
work hard, stand out, be exceptional.
Then we grew up and found out:
just waking up on time, eating actual meals,
paying rent on schedule,
not falling apart on Sunday night —
is a small daily war you have to win every time.

What you call 'I haven't accomplished anything'
is actually:
you didn't quit,
you didn't check out,
you didn't turn your life into a news headline,
you didn't infect the people around you with your anxiety.

None of this gets applauded,
but every single one of them costs you something.

So next time you start to say 'how have I done nothing at my age,'
try switching it to —
'at my age,
I'm still running a functional life.
That's already a lot.'

Ordinary isn't failure.
Ordinary is the most quietly impressive thing you can pull off right now.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's started asking 'what have I actually done with these years' — the fact that you're still here is the answer
variations: Advanced: before you write the 'what I didn't do this year' list, write a 'how many times I didn't fall apart this year' list — you'll notice you've been winning the whole time

items.sourceNote: PINKVILLA韓劇金句回顧，《鬼怪》「沒見過你，就不會知道世界這麼美」原創改編

zh-tw.content:
《鬼怪》那句台詞——
「如果沒有遇見你，
我不會知道這個世界這麼美。」

聽起來像情話，
其實是這部劇最深的一個觀察：

你以為你是因為遇見了那個人才開始愛這個世界，
其實不是。
是那個人讓你重新打開眼睛——
看到便利商店的燈光原來這麼溫暖、
下雨天原來有自己的味道、
冬天的圍巾原來可以讓人想哭、
地鐵的廣播原來也有它的節奏。

那些東西一直都在，
只是你以前沒看見。

所以遇見對的人，
最重要的禮物不是「他陪你做了什麼」，
是——
他讓你開始注意到自己的人生。

就算他後來離開了，
那個被打開的世界不會關回去。
你已經學會看了。

這就是為什麼，
有些人就算只待過一陣子，
也值得感謝。
他改變了你看世界的方式，
而你會帶著那個方式繼續走下去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個離開過你的人——不一定要他知道，但你可以在心裡好好說一次「謝謝你來過」
variations: 進階版：那個讓你重新看見世界的人，不一定是戀人，可能是朋友、老師、甚至一隻貓——回頭去謝謝他們其中一個

en.content:
That line from Goblin —
"If I hadn't met you,
I wouldn't have known how beautiful this world is."

It sounds like a love line,
but it's actually the show's deepest observation:

You think you started loving the world because you met that person.
You didn't.
That person made you open your eyes again —
you noticed the convenience store light was actually warm,
rainy days had their own smell,
a winter scarf could almost make you cry,
the subway announcements had their own rhythm.

All of it was already there.
You just hadn't seen it.

So when you meet the right person,
the most important gift isn't 'what they did with you.'
It's —
they got you to start noticing your own life.

Even if they leave later,
that opened world doesn't close back up.
You've already learned how to see.

That's why
some people, even if they only stayed a while,
are worth being grateful for.
They changed how you see the world,
and you carry that with you from now on.
en.editorNote: For the one who left — they don't have to know, but you can quietly say 'thank you for showing up' to yourself
variations: Advanced: the person who made you see the world differently doesn't have to be a partner — could be a friend, a teacher, even a cat. Go thank one of them

items.sourceNote: The Daily Jagran《背著善宰跑》金句整理，「在每一個輪迴裡都會愛你」原創改編

zh-tw.content:
《背著善宰跑》最讓人受不了的那句話——
「不管是哪一個輪迴，
我都會去找到你。
在每一個輪迴裡，
我都會愛上你。」

聽起來很浪漫，
但仔細想其實有一點殘忍：

他不是說「我會一直愛你」，
他是說「就算重來一次，
就算把我的記憶全部洗掉、
把你換個名字、換個樣子、
換到一個我根本不認識的城市——
我還是會走到你面前。」

這不是運氣，
這是一種選擇被刻進骨頭裡的程度。

人活到某個年紀會慢慢明白，
說「我愛你」很簡單，
但說「下輩子我還是會選你」——
那是已經知道全部會發生什麼事之後，
還願意再走一次的人才說得出來。

如果有一個人讓你想說這句話，
不要省。
趁這輩子還在，先跟他說一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個你覺得「下輩子還想遇到」的人——不一定要等到下輩子才講
variations: 進階版：不只情人——朋友、家人、貓，都可以是「下輩子再見」的對象。今天就挑一個告訴他

en.content:
The line from Lovely Runner that breaks people —
"No matter which lifetime it is,
I'll find you.
In every lifetime,
I'll fall in love with you."

It sounds romantic,
but think about it for a second and it's actually a little brutal:

He didn't say 'I'll love you forever.'
He said 'even if we start over,
even if my memory gets wiped,
even if your name changes, your face changes,
even if you're in a city I've never been to —
I'll still end up standing in front of you.'

That's not luck.
That's a choice carved into bone.

At some point you figure out:
saying 'I love you' is easy.
Saying 'I'd pick you again next life' —
after knowing exactly how everything is going to go down —
that's a different sentence entirely.

If someone in your life makes you want to say that,
don't save it.
While this life is still happening, tell them once.
en.editorNote: Send to the person you'd 'meet again next lifetime' — don't actually wait until next lifetime to say it
variations: Advanced: it doesn't have to be a partner — friends, family, your cat all qualify. Pick one and tell them today

items.sourceNote: 誠品迷《我的出走日記》台詞整理，「不假裝幸福、不假裝不幸、誠實以對」三原則原創改編

zh-tw.content:
《我的出走日記》裡的「解放同好會」訂了三個原則：
第一，不假裝幸福。
第二，不假裝不幸。
第三，誠實以對。

聽起來像廢話，
但其實大部分人連第一條都做不到。

你在公司會說「還好啊」，
在家會說「沒事啦」，
在群組會傳一個笑臉，
在個人頁面發一張看起來很滿足的食物照——

結果晚上洗完澡坐在床邊，
才終於可以小小聲地承認：
「其實我今天有一點不行。」

問題是——
如果連對自己都要演，
那這一天到底還剩多少是真的？

所以那三條原則，
不是給戲裡的角色設計的，
是給每一個下班還在演的我們設計的。

今天可以從小事開始：
不喜歡的飯，講出來；
累了，講出來；
想自己一個人吃午餐，講出來。

不假裝，
才有空間做真的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是回「還好」、但你知道他其實不太好的朋友——告訴他不用一直演
variations: 進階版：今天找一個人，把「還好」改成一句真話試試看，不用全部講完，講一句就好

en.content:
In My Liberation Notes, the 'Liberation Club' has three rules:
1. Don't fake being happy.
2. Don't fake being miserable.
3. Be honest.

Sounds obvious,
but most people can't even pull off rule one.

You say 'all good' at work,
'I'm fine' at home,
you send a smiley in the group chat,
you post a satisfied-looking food photo —

then at night, sitting on the edge of the bed after your shower,
you finally let yourself quietly admit:
'Honestly, today wasn't great.'

The problem is —
if you're performing even for yourself,
how much of this day is actually real?

Those three rules
weren't designed for the characters in the show.
They were designed for all of us still acting at 9pm.

Start small today:
the meal you don't like — say so.
You're tired — say so.
You want to eat lunch alone — say so.

No more faking it.
That's how you make room for the actual you.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who always replies 'I'm good' when you know they aren't — let them know they can stop performing
variations: Advanced: pick one person today and swap 'I'm fine' for one honest sentence. Doesn't have to be the whole truth, just one line

items.sourceNote: Marie Claire《黑暗榮耀》22句金句整理，文東恩「不想笑怕忘記要做什麼」原創改編

zh-tw.content:
《黑暗榮耀》裡文東恩說了一句很冷的話——
「你性格開朗這件事，
應該早點跟我說的，
因為我不想笑，
我怕笑多了，
會忘記自己要做什麼。」

第一次聽，覺得這個人好可怕。
第二次聽，懂了——

她不是不想開心，
她是知道：
一旦讓自己鬆下來，
那個一直撐著她走到今天的東西就會散掉。

人在追一個很重要的目標的時候，
常常會自己關掉一些感覺。
不是因為冷血，
是因為知道——
感情會分散注意力，
而她沒有那個餘裕。

這句話最痛的地方是：
它告訴你，有些人之所以看起來「不近人情」，
不是天生的，
是他們被生活逼到必須這樣，
才走得完接下來的路。

所以下次遇到一個看起來很冷的人，
先別急著評論。
他可能只是還沒走到可以開心的那一站。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在拼命追一個目標、看起來有點冷的朋友——不是要他軟下來，是讓他知道你看見了
variations: 進階版：等他走到那一站、把事情做完那天，記得去抱他一下，那時候他才有空哭

en.content:
There's a cold line in The Glory from Moon Dong-eun —
"You should've told me earlier
that you're a cheerful person.
Because I don't want to laugh.
I'm afraid if I laugh too much,
I'll forget what I came here to do."

First time you hear it, she sounds scary.
Second time, you get it —

She doesn't not want to be happy.
She knows:
the second she lets herself relax,
the thing that's been holding her up all this time will fall apart.

When someone is chasing something that matters,
they often turn off certain feelings on purpose.
Not because they're cold,
but because they know —
emotion is distracting,
and they can't afford the distraction.

The hardest part of this line:
some people look 'closed off'
not because they were born that way,
but because life pushed them into it,
so they could finish what they came to do.

Next time you meet someone who feels cold,
don't be quick to label them.
They might just not be at the stop where joy is allowed yet.
en.editorNote: For the friend currently locked into a big goal and looking a little shut down — not asking them to soften, just letting them know you see it
variations: Advanced: when they finally finish the thing, go give them a hug. That's when they finally have room to cry

items.sourceNote: Korean Binge《淚之女王》金句整理，「願意在你身邊修補一切」求婚台詞原創改編

zh-tw.content:
《淚之女王》裡他重新求婚那段，
沒有說「我會讓你幸福」，
他說的是——
「我沒辦法保證以後一定都很好，
我也沒辦法保證我們不會因為小事再吵起來。
但我可以保證一件事：
我會留在你身邊，
不管發生什麼，壞掉的我來修。」

這句話之所以讓人哭，
是因為它不像求婚——
更像道歉。

大人的承諾不是「我們不會吵」，
是「就算吵了，我也不走」。
大人的愛不是「我會給你完美的生活」，
是「不完美的部分，我來想辦法」。

大概也是這個年紀才慢慢看懂：
那個願意陪你修東西的人，
比那個一直承諾「以後都不會壞」的人，
值得太多。

因為一個只是嘴上講，
另一個是真的有打算留下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在猶豫一段關係要不要繼續的朋友——重點不是它有沒有壞掉，是對方願不願意一起修
variations: 進階版：你也可以反過來問自己——你是那個願意留下來一起修的人嗎？兩邊都得是，這段關係才修得起來

en.content:
In Queen of Tears, when he proposes the second time,
he doesn't say 'I'll make you happy.'
He says —
"I can't promise it'll all be good from here.
I can't promise we won't fight over stupid things again.
But I can promise one thing:
I'll stay by your side.
Whatever breaks, I'll fix it."

The reason this line wrecks people
is because it's not really a proposal —
it's closer to an apology.

A grown-up promise isn't 'we won't fight.'
It's 'even if we do, I'm not leaving.'
Grown-up love isn't 'I'll give you a perfect life.'
It's 'the imperfect parts — I'll figure them out.'

It takes a certain age to finally see it:
the person who'll sit there and fix things with you
is worth so much more
than the person who keeps promising 'nothing will ever break.'

One is just talking.
The other is actually planning to stay.
en.editorNote: For the friend on the fence about a relationship — the question isn't whether something broke, it's whether the other person will help fix it
variations: Advanced: flip it on yourself too — are you the one who stays and fixes? Both sides need to be, or the repair doesn't hold

items.sourceNote: POPBEE 2026治癒系韓劇台詞推薦，「情緒生病像感冒一樣正常」原創改編

zh-tw.content:
近幾年韓劇最大的進步，
是它終於敢這樣寫：
「情緒生病，跟感冒一樣正常。
感冒要吃藥、要休息、要跟公司請假，
那情緒生病為什麼不行？」

小時候被教的是——
「不要哭」、「忍一下就好」、
「想開一點」、「別人比你慘多了」。

結果長大之後，
我們變成一群很會處理感冒、
但完全不知道怎麼處理情緒的人。

胃痛你會掛號，
但心痛你會自己撐三個月。
發燒你會請病假，
但焦慮到睡不著你照樣去上班。

韓劇現在敢拍這些，
是因為它知道——
觀眾需要有人替他們講一次：
「你那個不是矯情、不是脆弱，
那是真的不舒服。
值得被當一回事。」

所以今天如果你有點不對勁，
不要急著解釋「應該還好」。
先承認它，
再決定要不要去處理它。

感冒不會因為你假裝沒事就自己好。
情緒也是。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明很不舒服、卻一直說「我沒事」的朋友——你不用假裝堅強，先承認再說
variations: 進階版：今天為自己「請一次情緒病假」，可以是半小時、一杯咖啡、一段散步——讓自己正式承認「我現在有點不行」

en.content:
The biggest shift in K-dramas the last few years
is they finally have the guts to write this:
"Getting sick emotionally is as normal as a cold.
You take medicine for a cold, you rest, you call out from work —
so why not for this?"

As kids we were taught —
'don't cry,' 'just push through,'
'try to think positive,' 'other people have it worse.'

So we grew up into a generation
that handles colds very well
and has absolutely no idea what to do with emotions.

Stomach pain — you book a doctor.
Heart pain — you white-knuckle it for three months.
Fever — you call out.
Anxiety so bad you can't sleep — you still go to work.

K-dramas can write this now
because they know —
the audience needs someone to say it once for them:
'What you're feeling isn't dramatic, isn't weakness.
It's real discomfort.
It deserves to be taken seriously.'

So today, if something feels off,
don't rush to explain it away with 'I'm probably fine.'
Acknowledge it first.
Then decide what to do about it.

A cold doesn't go away because you pretend it isn't there.
Neither does this.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's clearly not okay but keeps saying 'I'm fine' — you don't have to perform strength, start by admitting it
variations: Advanced: take one 'emotional sick day' today — half an hour, a coffee, a walk. Make it official: 'I'm not okay right now, and that's allowed'

items.sourceNote: 2025年話題韓劇親情主題討論，母女關係台詞觀察，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
今年最戳人的韓劇台詞，
不是男女主角在雨中告白，
而是媽媽對長大的女兒說：

「我這輩子最大的成就，
不是把妳養大，
是讓妳變成一個
不需要為了討我開心
而委屈自己的人。」

看到這句的時候，
你會突然很想打電話回家，
但又不知道要說什麼，
最後只傳了一句：「在幹嘛？」

那句「在幹嘛」，
其實是「我想你了」的台灣版翻譯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明很想家、卻只會傳「在幹嘛」給媽媽的朋友——這就是我們表達想念的方式
variations: 進階版：今天真的打一通電話回家，不用有事，就聊三分鐘——你會發現掛掉之後心裡有個地方變暖了

en.content:
The K-drama line that wrecked everyone this year
wasn't a rain-soaked confession between leads.
It was a mother telling her grown daughter:

"The biggest thing I ever did
wasn't raising you.
It was raising you into someone
who doesn't have to shrink herself
just to keep me happy."

The second you read that,
you want to call home —
but you don't know what to say,
so you just text: "what are you up to?"

That "what are you up to"
is the translated version of "I miss you."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who clearly misses home but only ever texts their mom 'what are you up to' — that's how we say it
variations: Advanced: actually call home today. No reason needed, just three minutes. You'll notice a spot inside you warms up after you hang up.

items.sourceNote: Dcard韓劇板字幕追劇行為討論，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
看韓劇看久了會出現一種後遺症：

你開始覺得自己聽得懂韓文。

字幕都還沒跑出來，
你就跟著喊「歐摩」、「真的假的」、「我愛你」，
然後很滿意地點點頭，
覺得自己根本不需要字幕。

直到字幕真的消失三秒鐘，
你瞬間清醒，
意識到自己其實一個字都沒聽懂，
只是把語氣背起來而已。

「會的韓文」和「會背的語氣」，
是兩回事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個追劇追到以為自己會韓文、嘴上一直「歐摩歐摩」的朋友——讓他笑著認清現實
variations: 更慘的版本：你跟同事自信地說「我看韓劇學了不少韓文」，結果去韓國旅遊時，唯一派上用場的只有「卡姆撒哈密達」

en.content:
Watch enough K-dramas and you develop a side effect:

you start believing you understand Korean.

Before the subtitles even load,
you're already saying "omo," "jinjja," "saranghae"
right along with them,
nodding to yourself,
convinced you don't even need the captions.

Then the subtitles actually drop for three seconds,
and you snap back to reality:
you understood literally nothing.
You just memorized the tone.

"Knowing Korean"
and "memorizing the intonation"
are not the same skill.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who thinks binge-watching taught them Korean and won't stop saying 'omo omo' — let them laugh and face the truth
variations: Worse version: you confidently tell coworkers 'I picked up a lot of Korean from dramas,' then on your Korea trip the only phrase you actually use is 'kamsahamnida.'

items.sourceNote: UDN女子漾韓劇人生哲理金句整理，職場與成長主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句話一直被截圖轉發：

「不要因為害怕受傷，
就提前把自己關起來。
你以為這樣很安全，
其實你只是錯過了
所有本來會發生的好事。」

聽起來很雞湯，
但仔細想想——
你拒絕的那場聚會、
沒去面試的那份工作、
不敢傳出去的那則訊息，
哪一個不是因為「怕」？

安全感有時候不是保護你，
是把你困在原地。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是用「我這樣比較安全」當理由、其實是在逃避的朋友——溫柔但要讓他聽進去
variations: 進階版：今天做一件你「有點怕但其實想做」的小事就好——報名那堂課、傳那則訊息、舉手說那句話。怕和想，可以同時存在。

en.content:
There's a K-drama line that keeps getting screenshotted:

"Don't lock yourself away in advance
just because you're scared of getting hurt.
You think it keeps you safe,
but really you're just missing
every good thing that was supposed to happen."

Sounds like a fortune cookie,
until you actually think about it —
the gathering you turned down,
the job you didn't interview for,
the text you never dared to send.
Which of those wasn't fear?

Sometimes safety isn't protecting you.
It's just keeping you stuck.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps saying 'this is safer for me' when they're really just avoiding — gentle, but make it land
variations: Advanced: do one small thing today that scares you a little but you actually want — sign up, send the text, raise your hand. Scared and wanting can coexist.

items.sourceNote: POPBEE韓劇追劇時間管理現象觀察，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
追韓劇的人都很擅長一種數學：

凌晨一點，看完一集。
你心想：「最後一集了，看完就睡。」

結果片尾留了個超大的伏筆，
你大喊：「不行，這怎麼睡得著。」

於是「再一集」變成「再三集」，
「就睡」變成「天亮」。

隔天上班頂著黑眼圈，
同事問你怎麼了，
你只能說：「沒事，就……劇情需要。」

世界上最遠的距離，
是「最後一集」和「真正睡覺」之間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次都說「看完這集就睡」、然後追到天亮的朋友——他需要被點名
variations: 進階版：今晚設一個真的會響的鬧鐘，到點就暫停，把伏筆留到明天——相信我，劇情明天還在，但你的黑眼圈不會自己消失

en.content:
K-drama fans are weirdly good at one kind of math:

1 AM, you finish an episode.
You think: "last one, then I sleep."

Then the credits drop a massive cliffhanger
and you yell: "nope, no way I'm sleeping after that."

So "one more" becomes "three more,"
and "then sleep" becomes "then sunrise."

Next day at work, raccoon eyes,
a coworker asks what happened,
and all you can say is: "nothing, it's just… the plot needed it."

The longest distance in the world
is between "last episode"
and "actually going to sleep."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who says 'just this episode then bed' and watches till sunrise every single time — call them out
variations: Advanced: set an alarm that actually goes off tonight, pause when it rings, save the cliffhanger for tomorrow. Trust me, the plot will still be there. Your eye bags won't fix themselves.

items.sourceNote: beauty321韓劇友情主題場景整理，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇最會拍的，其實不是愛情，
是那種一句話都不用說的友情。

你心情很差，
朋友沒問你怎麼了，
只是默默放了一碗熱湯在你面前，
坐在旁邊陪你發呆。

劇裡那句台詞是這樣的：
「我不一定懂你發生了什麼，
但我可以坐在這裡，
直到你想說話為止。」

真正的陪伴，
從來不是給建議，
是讓你知道：你難過的時候，
旁邊有個位子，一直是你的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是默默陪你、什麼都不問的朋友——讓他知道那碗湯你一直記得
variations: 進階版：今天主動坐到那個看起來不太好的朋友旁邊，不用問，不用給建議，就陪著——有時候在場本身就是答案

en.content:
What K-dramas film best isn't romance.
It's the kind of friendship that needs no words.

You're in a terrible mood,
and your friend doesn't ask what's wrong.
They just quietly set a bowl of hot soup in front of you
and sit beside you while you stare into space.

The line in the show goes:
"I might not understand what happened to you,
but I can sit right here
until you feel like talking."

Real company
was never about giving advice.
It's letting someone know:
when you're hurting, there's a seat next to you,
and it's always been yours.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who quietly stays with you and never pries — let them know you still remember that bowl of soup
variations: Advanced: today, go sit next to the friend who seems off. Don't ask, don't advise, just be there. Sometimes showing up is the whole answer.

items.sourceNote: POPBEE 2026治癒系韓劇台詞推薦，平凡人生主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
長大之後才發現，
光是把日子過成「普通」，
就已經用掉我全部的力氣。

小時候以為長大會變很厲害，
結果現在的目標是：
準時起床、好好吃飯、
帳單不要遲繳、別在深夜emo太久。

韓劇裡有句話我記了好久：
「承認自己只想當個平凡人，
一點都不丟臉。」

不是每個人都要發光，
有時候能穩穩地活著，
就已經很了不起了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總覺得自己「不夠好」、把普通生活活成壓力的朋友——告訴他活著本身就值得被肯定
variations: 進階版：今天先不要逼自己變得更好，只要把今天好好過完就夠了——明天的你，會謝謝今天沒有崩潰的你

en.content:
Growing up, I figured out
that just keeping life "normal"
eats up every bit of energy I have.

As a kid I thought I'd turn out impressive.
Now my actual goals are:
wake up on time, eat real meals,
pay bills before they're late,
and not spiral at 2 AM.

There's a K-drama line I've held onto:
"There's nothing shameful
about admitting you just want to be ordinary."

Not everyone has to shine.
Sometimes just staying steady, staying alive,
is already a huge deal.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who feels 'not good enough' and turns ordinary life into pressure — remind them that simply being here counts
variations: Advanced: today, don't force yourself to be better. Just get through today. Tomorrow-you will thank today-you for not falling apart.

items.sourceNote: POPBEE 2026治癒系韓劇台詞，幸福主導權主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇教會我一件很殘酷但很真的事：

你的快樂，只有你自己給得起。

等別人記得你的生日才覺得被愛、
等對方先傳訊息才敢開心、
等老闆一句肯定才覺得自己有用——

你把幸福的開關，
交到了一個不一定會幫你打開的人手上。

劇裡那句話是這樣說的：
「不要把決定你快不快樂的權力，
交給別人。」

從今天開始，
那個開關，自己拿回來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是在等別人給自己快樂、卻常常失望的朋友——提醒他幸福的主導權一直在自己手上
variations: 進階版：今天做一件「只為了讓自己開心」的小事，不用問別人意見，不用等任何人同意——那叫把開關拿回來

en.content:
K-dramas taught me something brutal but true:

Your happiness is something only you can hand yourself.

Waiting for someone to remember your birthday to feel loved,
waiting for them to text first before you let yourself be happy,
waiting for your boss's approval to feel useful —

you've handed the switch to your own joy
to a person who might never flip it on.

The line in the show goes:
"Don't give anyone else
the power to decide whether you're happy."

Starting today,
take that switch back.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps waiting on others for happiness and keeps getting let down — remind them the controls were always theirs
variations: Advanced: today do one small thing purely to make yourself happy. Don't ask for opinions, don't wait for permission. That's taking the switch back.

items.sourceNote: TVBS女人我最大《今生也請多指教》台詞整理，假裝懂事主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
有句韓劇台詞我看到的時候愣住了：

「人到死都不會真的懂事，
大家都只是裝出來的而已。」

原來不是只有我。
原來那些看起來很成熟、
什麼事都處理得很好的大人，
其實心裡也一團亂，
只是學會了在別人面前裝鎮定。

沒有人是真的長大了，
我們只是越來越會假裝。

所以你今天覺得自己很狼狽，
沒關係——
旁邊那個看起來很穩的人，
搞不好比你還慌。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個覺得「別人都好成熟、只有我很狼狽」的朋友——讓他知道大家其實都在裝
variations: 進階版：下次看到那個超有條理的同事，記得他可能也是早上才在廁所深呼吸三次才出來的——你沒有比較差，大家都在硬撐

en.content:
A K-drama line once stopped me cold:

"Nobody actually grows wise before they die.
Everyone's just faking it."

Turns out it wasn't just me.
Turns out the adults who look so put-together,
who seem to handle everything,
are a mess inside too —
they've just learned to look calm in front of people.

Nobody really grew up.
We just got better at pretending.

So if you feel like a disaster today,
it's okay —
that person next to you who looks so steady
is probably more panicked than you are.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who thinks 'everyone else is so mature and only I'm a mess' — let them know everyone is faking it
variations: Advanced: next time you see that hyper-organized coworker, remember they might've taken three deep breaths in the bathroom this morning too. You're not behind. Everyone's white-knuckling it.

items.sourceNote: VOGUE HK《低谷醫生》治癒金句整理，照顧自己主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
《低谷醫生》裡有句話，
剛好戳中正在硬撐的你：

「不要太在意別人的眼光，
先把自己照顧好。
今天的你要先好好的，
才有辦法去接住明天的自己。」

我們常常把自己排到最後，
先顧工作、先顧別人、先顧那些
其實沒那麼在乎你的人。

結果輪到自己的時候，
已經沒電了。

你不是機器，
偶爾關機、充電、什麼都不做，
不叫懶，叫做活著的維修時間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是先顧好所有人、唯獨忘記自己的朋友——提醒他先把自己照顧好
variations: 進階版：今天排一段「什麼都不為任何人做」的時間，哪怕只有半小時——那不是浪費，那是讓你撐到明天的充電

en.content:
There's a line in Doctor Slump
that lands right on whoever's white-knuckling it:

"Don't worry so much about how others see you.
Take care of yourself first.
You have to be okay today
so you can catch tomorrow's version of you."

We keep putting ourselves last —
work first, other people first,
even people who don't really care about us first.

Then when it's finally our turn,
the battery's already dead.

You're not a machine.
Powering down sometimes, recharging, doing nothing —
that's not laziness.
That's the maintenance time being alive requires.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who takes care of everyone but forgets themselves — remind them to put their own oxygen mask on first
variations: Advanced: today, block out time to do nothing for anyone, even just thirty minutes. That's not wasted time. That's the recharge that gets you to tomorrow.

items.sourceNote: Korean Binge 韓劇愛情台詞整理，選擇你主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡最讓人心動的告白，
從來不是「我愛你」三個字，
是那種「我選你」的篤定：

「如果有一天，
我必須做一個很殘忍的選擇，
我會選你。
因為失去我愛的人，
比失去我自己還要痛。」

愛你，不是激情上頭的衝動，
是在所有理智都清醒的時候，
還是把你放在最前面。

那不是一時的心動，
那是一種——「無論如何都是你」的決定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在猶豫要不要認真對待一段感情的人——真正的愛是清醒時依然選擇你
variations: 進階版：愛情裡最浪漫的不是「我好喜歡你」，是吵完架、累到不行、看清彼此缺點之後，還願意說一句「明天還要一起」

en.content:
The most heart-stopping confessions in K-dramas
were never the words "I love you."
It's that quiet certainty of "I choose you":

"If the day ever comes
when I have to make a cruel choice,
I'll choose you.
Because losing the person I love
hurts more than losing myself."

Loving you isn't a rush of passion.
It's choosing you first
even when every rational part of me is wide awake.

That's not a fleeting flutter.
That's a decision —
"no matter what, it's you."
en.editorNote: Send to someone hesitating over whether to take a relationship seriously — real love is still choosing you when the head is clear
variations: Advanced: the most romantic thing in love isn't 'I really like you.' It's saying 'let's do tomorrow together' after the fight, after the exhaustion, after seeing each other's flaws clearly.

items.sourceNote: UDN女子漾《低谷醫生》治癒金句整理，倒下休息主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句話，
是給每個撐到快斷氣的人聽的：

「既然都倒下了，
那就先躺著休息一下吧。」

我們從小被教的是：
跌倒要馬上爬起來、
哭完要馬上振作、
累了也要假裝沒事。

但其實啊，
倒下不是失敗，
是身體在替你喊停。

地板沒有那麼可怕，
躺一下，看看天花板，
等你想站起來的時候再站，
沒有人在計時。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明已經很累、卻還在硬撐的人——告訴他停下來不是認輸，是補給
variations: 進階版：你不需要每天都很努力。有些日子，把自己餵飽、好好睡一覺，就已經是這一天最了不起的成就了

en.content:
There's a line in K-dramas
meant for everyone running on empty:

"Since you've already fallen down,
just lie there and rest for a bit."

We were raised to believe:
get up the second you trip,
snap out of it the second you stop crying,
pretend you're fine even when you're wrecked.

But here's the thing —
falling isn't failure.
It's your body calling a timeout for you.

The floor isn't that scary.
Lie down, look at the ceiling,
get up when you actually want to.
Nobody's running a clock.
en.editorNote: Send to someone who's clearly exhausted but still pushing through — let them know stopping isn't quitting, it's refueling
variations: Advanced: you don't have to crush it every single day. Some days, feeding yourself and getting real sleep is the most impressive thing you'll do.

items.sourceNote: POPBEE 2026治癒系韓劇台詞推薦，幸福不問別人主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇問了一個很簡單、
卻很多人答不出來的問題：

「你的快樂，
為什麼要問別人？」

我們太習慣了——
發了文要看有多少人按讚、
做了決定要先問大家覺得怎樣、
連自己開不開心，
都要靠別人的反應來確認。

可是幸福這件事，
從來沒有外包的選項。

沒有人比你更知道，
什麼能讓你睡得安穩、
醒來不慌。

把那支遙控器，
收回自己手上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是在意別人眼光、活得很累的朋友——提醒他快樂的決定權一直在自己手裡
variations: 進階版：你不需要全世界都認同你的選擇。只要那個選擇讓你晚上睡得著、早上起得來，它就是對的選擇

en.content:
A K-drama asked a question
that sounds simple
but leaves most people stuck:

"Your happiness —
why are you asking other people about it?"

We've gotten too used to it —
checking how many likes the post got,
running every decision past everyone first,
even needing someone else's reaction
to confirm whether we're happy.

But happiness
was never something you could outsource.

No one knows better than you
what lets you sleep easy
and wake up without that knot in your chest.

Take the remote back.
It was always yours to hold.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's worn out from caring too much what others think — remind them the call on their own happiness has always been theirs
variations: Advanced: you don't need the whole world to approve of your choice. If it lets you sleep at night and get up in the morning, it's the right one.

items.sourceNote: Korean Binge 韓劇愛情台詞整理，思念與飢餓比喻，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇把「想你」這件事，
寫得連你自己都分不清楚：

「我是因為餓了才想你，
還是因為想你才覺得餓？」

喜歡一個人到某個程度，
會出現一種很荒謬的混亂——
吃飯想到他、
走路想到他、
明明在忙，腦袋還是自己飄過去。

你開始懷疑
自己是不是身體出了什麼問題，
後來才發現，
那不是毛病，
那是喜歡。

而且是
連肚子餓都怪到他頭上的那種喜歡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個最近一直心不在焉、嘴上不承認自己戀愛了的朋友——幫他確診
variations: 進階版：判斷你是不是真的喜歡一個人，很簡單——看到好笑的東西，第一個想分享的人是誰，答案就藏在那裡

en.content:
A K-drama wrote "missing you"
in a way that even confuses the person feeling it:

"Am I thinking of you because I'm hungry,
or am I hungry because I'm thinking of you?"

When you like someone past a certain point,
this ridiculous kind of confusion shows up —
you think of them while eating,
while walking,
your brain drifts off to them mid-task.

You start wondering
if something's medically wrong with you.
Then you realize
it's not a glitch.
It's a crush.

The kind
where you even blame your hunger on them.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's been distracted lately and won't admit they've caught feelings — give them the diagnosis
variations: Advanced: an easy test for whether you actually like someone — when something funny happens, who's the first person you want to tell? The answer's hiding right there.

items.sourceNote: TVBS女人我最大《今生也請多指教》人生台詞整理，裝懂主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇講了一句很殘忍、
卻很解脫的真話：

「人到死都不會真的懂事，
大家都只是裝出來的。」

你以為長大之後，
就會有一個瞬間突然什麼都懂了、
所有焦慮都消失、
每件事都知道怎麼處理。

結果三十歲、四十歲，
還是常常一邊慌、
一邊假裝鎮定地把事情做完。

但你知道嗎，
那些看起來很有把握的大人，
其實也都在裝。

所以別再等那個「終於成熟」的瞬間了——
它不會來，
而你已經做得夠好了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老覺得自己「還不夠成熟、還沒準備好」的朋友——告訴他沒有人準備好，大家都是邊做邊學
variations: 進階版：所謂的大人，不是不再害怕，是學會了害怕的時候，還是把該做的事做完

en.content:
A K-drama said something brutal
that somehow sets you free:

"Nobody truly has it figured out, even at the end.
Everyone's just faking it."

You assumed that after growing up,
there'd be one moment where it all clicked —
the anxiety vanishes,
you suddenly know how to handle everything.

Then you're thirty, forty,
still panicking quietly
while pretending to be calm enough to get things done.

But here's the secret:
those grown-ups who look so sure of themselves?
They're faking it too.

So stop waiting for the "finally mature" moment —
it isn't coming,
and you're already doing just fine.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps thinking they're 'not mature enough, not ready yet' — tell them nobody's ready, everyone's figuring it out as they go
variations: Advanced: being a grown-up doesn't mean you stop being scared. It means you learned to do what needs doing even while you're scared.

items.sourceNote: POPBEE韓劇金句整理，婚姻與陪伴主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡最會撩的告白，
從來不是「我愛你」三個字。

是那種——
「和你在一起的每一天都很耀眼。
天氣好的時候、
天氣不好的時候、
天氣剛剛好的時候，
全都很耀眼。」

你以為它在說天氣，
其實它在說：
不管你今天是什麼樣子，
我都覺得有你在的這一天，
值得記住。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個你想說「不管你狀態好不好我都想陪著你」的人——比直接說我愛你更戳心
variations: 更日常的版本：和你在一起，連塞車、下雨、便利商店買咖啡，這些超無聊的事都變得想記下來

en.content:
The smoothest K-drama confession
is never the three words "I love you."

It's the kind that goes:
"Every day with you was dazzling.
The days the weather was nice,
the days it wasn't,
the days it was just right —
all of them, dazzling."

You think it's about the weather.
It's really saying:
no matter what you're like today,
any day with you in it
is worth remembering.
en.editorNote: Send to the person you want to tell 'good day or bad, I just want to be there with you' — it lands harder than a plain 'I love you'
variations: The everyday version: with you, even traffic, rain, and grabbing coffee at the convenience store somehow feel worth writing down.

items.sourceNote: Pinkvilla韓劇金句，自我價值與愛得多主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇講過一句話，
戳破了一個我們從小被教的迷思：

「愛一個人愛得比對方多，
不是丟臉的事，
那是值得驕傲的事。」

我們總被提醒：
不要先告白、
不要先已讀回、
不要讓對方知道你比較在乎，
好像先動心的人就輸了。

但其實能毫無保留地喜歡一個人，
是一種很多人一輩子都學不會的勇敢。

你不是輸了，
你只是比較敢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總覺得「先喜歡上就輸了」、不敢先付出的朋友——告訴他先動心不丟臉
variations: 進階版：愛得多的人不是傻，是他知道感情不是比賽，沒有誰要贏過誰

en.content:
A K-drama said something
that shatters a myth we're taught young:

"Loving someone more than they love you
isn't embarrassing.
It's something to be proud of."

We're always warned:
don't confess first,
don't reply too fast,
don't let them see you care more —
as if whoever falls first loses.

But being able to like someone
with nothing held back
is a kind of courage most people never learn.

You didn't lose.
You were just braver.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who thinks 'whoever falls first loses' and is scared to give first — remind them falling first isn't shameful
variations: Advanced: the one who loves more isn't a fool. They just figured out love isn't a contest where someone has to win.

items.sourceNote: KORB韓劇愛情台詞，時間感受主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇有一句台詞，
短到不行，卻很可怕地準：

「沒有你的那三十幾年，
我過得飛快；
認識你之後的每一天，
卻長得像一輩子。」

喜歡一個人最明顯的證據，
不是心跳加速，
是你的時間感壞掉了——

等他回訊息的十分鐘像一小時，
和他在一起的三小時像三分鐘。

如果你最近覺得時間怪怪的，
那可能不是你的錯，
是某個人住進了你的日子裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個最近老說「跟某人在一起時間過超快」的朋友——幫他確認一下他是不是喜歡上了
variations: 反過來的版本：如果跟一個人在一起，你一直偷看時間，那也是答案，只是是另一種答案

en.content:
There's a K-drama line
so short it shouldn't work, but it's scarily accurate:

"The thirty-odd years before you
went by in a blink;
every day since I met you
feels like it lasts forever."

The clearest proof you like someone
isn't the racing heartbeat.
It's that your sense of time breaks —

ten minutes waiting for their text feels like an hour,
three hours with them feels like three minutes.

If time's been feeling strange lately,
it might not be your fault.
Someone just moved into your days.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps saying 'time flies when I'm with this person' — help them confirm whether they've caught feelings
variations: The flip side: if you keep sneaking glances at the clock when you're with someone, that's an answer too — just a different one.

items.sourceNote: Pinkvilla韓劇人生金句，把握當下說出口主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇講過一段很催的話：

「時間會一直往前走，
所以時間最後一定會帶來告別，
也一定會留下遺憾。
如果你愛一個人，
趁現在就告訴他——
在你還來得及、
還沒變成後悔之前。」

我們總想等一個更好的時機：
等我準備好、
等他狀態好一點、
等氣氛對了再說。

但很多話沒說出口，
不是因為不愛，
是因為一直以為「還有時間」。

你想跟某個人說的那句話，
今天就去說吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直把「想說的話」往後拖的朋友——提醒他別等到變成遺憾才開口
variations: 溫柔版：不一定要是告白，一句「謝謝你那時候在」「我其實很在乎你」，也都別等了

en.content:
A K-drama said something that hits hard:

"Time keeps moving forward,
so time will eventually bring farewells,
and it always leaves regrets behind.
If you love someone,
tell them now —
while you still can,
before it turns into something you wish you'd said."

We always wait for a better moment:
when I'm ready,
when they're in a better place,
when the mood is right.

But the words we never said
weren't held back because we didn't care.
We just kept assuming there was still time.

That thing you want to tell someone?
Go say it today.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps putting off 'the thing they want to say' — remind them not to wait until it becomes a regret
variations: The gentle version: it doesn't have to be a confession. 'Thank you for being there back then,' 'I actually care about you a lot' — don't wait on those either.

items.sourceNote: When Life Gives You Tangerines（苦盡柑來遇見你），大人也會被燙傷主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
小時候以為，長大之後，
手會長繭、心也會長繭，
再痛的事都能笑著扛過去。

結果長大了才發現：
手是長繭了，
心一點都沒有。

每一次受傷還是一樣痛，
每一次失望還是一樣難過，
只是我學會了
在別人面前裝沒事。

原來不是長大就不痛了，
是長大之後，
沒有人能替你喊痛了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是把「我沒事」掛在嘴邊、但你知道他其實很累的朋友——讓他知道有人看得見
variations: 更貼心的版本：你不用在我面前裝沒事，至少在我這裡，你可以喊痛

en.content:
As a kid, I thought that once you grew up,
your hands would callous over,
and so would your heart —
that nothing would really hurt anymore.

Then I grew up and found out:
the hands got calloused, sure.
The heart never did.

Every wound still stings the same.
Every letdown still aches the same.
The only thing I learned
was how to look fine in front of people.

Turns out growing up doesn't stop the pain.
It just means no one
gets to flinch on your behalf anymore.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who always says 'I'm fine' but you can tell they're worn out — let them know someone sees it
variations: The softer version: you don't have to look fine in front of me. With me, at least, you're allowed to say it hurts.

items.sourceNote: When Life Gives You Tangerines，父母與孩子各自掛念主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句，看完整個人安靜下來：

「父母一輩子都在想，
自己沒能給孩子的那些；
孩子一輩子都在記，
自己沒能得到的那些。」

所以一邊覺得虧欠，
一邊覺得委屈，
兩個人明明最愛彼此，
卻常常用最笨的方式錯過。

下次回家，
別急著翻舊帳，
他們給的，
其實一直比你記得的多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個跟爸媽容易吵、但其實很愛他們的朋友——提醒他別只記得沒得到的
variations: 行動版：今天打通電話回家，什麼事都不用講，就說一句「我最近很好，你們別擔心」

en.content:
A K-drama line that made the whole room go quiet:

"Parents spend their whole lives
dwelling on what they couldn't give.
Children spend their whole lives
dwelling on what they didn't get."

So one side carries guilt,
the other carries hurt,
and the two people who love each other most
keep missing each other the clumsiest way.

Next time you go home,
don't reach for the old grievances.
They gave more
than you remember.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who argues with their parents easily but loves them deeply — a nudge to stop counting only what they didn't get
variations: The action version: call home today. Don't bring anything up. Just say, 'I've been doing well, don't worry about me.'

items.sourceNote: When Life Gives You Tangerines，撐住就能再呼吸主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一段話，
適合在很撐不住的那天聽：

「總有一天，
日子會難到你覺得走不下去。
那種時候，
不要躺著不動，
要用盡全力掙扎——
告訴自己：我不會死，
我一定要活著撐過去。
然後你就會發現，
你又能呼吸了。」

低潮不是叫你堅強，
是叫你別停手。
撐住，
下一口氣就在前面。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在硬撐、快要放棄的朋友——不用講道理，把這段話給他就好
variations: 簡短版：你現在覺得喘不過氣，是因為你還在水裡掙扎，這代表你還沒放棄——這樣很好

en.content:
There's a K-drama line
made for the days you can barely hold on:

"One day life will get so hard
you'll feel like you can't go on.
When that day comes,
don't just lie there.
Struggle with everything you've got —
tell yourself: I won't die,
I have to survive this no matter what.
And then you'll realize
you can breathe again."

A low point isn't asking you to be strong.
It's asking you not to let go.
Hold on.
The next breath is right there.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's white-knuckling it and about to give up — skip the advice, just give them this
variations: The short version: if you feel like you can't breathe right now, it's because you're still fighting in the water. That means you haven't given up. That's good.

items.sourceNote: Bon Appetit Your Majesty（2025），想你還是餓了主題告白，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇男主角講過一句很犯規的告白：

「我搞不清楚——
是因為想你，所以覺得餓，
還是因為餓，所以一直想你。」

聽起來很傻，
但喜歡一個人的時候，
真的會這樣：

吃飯想到他、
走路想到他、
明明在忙別的事，
腦子還是繞回他身上。

所謂喜歡，
大概就是怎麼分心，
都還是分到同一個人身上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個你一直在想、卻還沒說出口的人——當作一句不那麼直接、但很明顯的試探
variations: 更直接的版本：我已經放棄分辨是想你還是餓了，反正答案最後都是你

en.content:
A K-drama lead dropped this unfairly good confession:

"I can't tell anymore —
am I hungry because I'm thinking of you,
or thinking of you because I'm hungry?"

It sounds silly,
but when you actually like someone,
this is exactly how it goes:

you think of them at dinner,
you think of them walking,
you're busy with something else entirely
and your mind still loops back to them.

Liking someone is basically this:
no matter how you get distracted,
you keep getting distracted by the same person.
en.editorNote: Send to the person you keep thinking about but haven't told — an indirect but very obvious test of the waters
variations: The bolder version: I've given up trying to tell whether I'm thinking of you or just hungry. Either way the answer ends up being you.

items.sourceNote: Hometown Cha-Cha-Cha，人生沒有標準答案主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句，
適合送給很愛糾結的人：

「人生不是數學題，
沒有所謂的正確答案，
只有你怎麼把它過下去。」

我們總想找一個對的選擇：
選對工作、選對城市、
選對那個人——
好像選錯了，整個人生就毀了。

但其實沒有那條一定對的路，
是你走著走著、
把它走成了對的。

別再算了，
答案是活出來的，
不是選出來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個卡在重大選擇、怕選錯而動不了的朋友——告訴他沒有標準答案，走下去才有答案
variations: 鼓勵版：你現在覺得選錯了，是因為還沒走到把它變對的那一段——再走遠一點看看

en.content:
There's a K-drama line
for everyone who overthinks every decision:

"Life isn't a math problem.
There's no correct answer —
only how you choose to live it out."

We keep hunting for the right choice:
the right job, the right city,
the right person —
as if one wrong pick ruins everything.

But there's no path that's right in advance.
You walk it,
and somewhere along the way
you make it the right one.

Stop calculating.
The answer is lived, not chosen.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend frozen by a big decision, scared of picking wrong — there's no answer key; the answer shows up by walking
variations: The encouraging version: if it feels like the wrong choice right now, it's just because you haven't walked far enough to make it right yet. Go a little further.

items.sourceNote: Queen of Tears（淚之女王），愛是一起撐過痛苦主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句，把愛情講得很不浪漫，
卻很真實：

「愛不是一直開心、一直說甜言蜜語。
愛是兩個人一起把痛苦撐過去，
是你選擇留下，
而不是轉身就走。」

剛在一起的時候，
誰都會說好聽的話。
難的是——
等熱戀退了、現實來了，
你還願不願意留在原地。

所謂愛，
不是最甜的那一刻，
是最難的那一刻你還在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給正在經歷感情低潮、開始懷疑「是不是不愛了」的朋友——告訴他願意留下本身就是愛
variations: 更短的版本：甜言蜜語誰都會說，難的是難關來的時候，那個人還站在你旁邊
婚姻版：結婚不是把最開心的人留在身邊，是把你願意一起撐過痛苦的人留在身邊

en.content:
A K-drama line describes love in the least romantic
yet most honest way:

"Love isn't always being happy and whispering sweet nothings.
Love is two people enduring the pain together —
choosing to stay
instead of turning and walking away."

At the start, anyone can say the pretty things.
The hard part is —
when the honeymoon fades and reality arrives,
whether you're still willing to stay put.

Love isn't the sweetest moment.
It's still being there in the hardest one.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend going through a rough patch who's starting to wonder 'have we stopped loving each other?' — being willing to stay is itself the love
variations: Shorter version: anyone can say sweet things. The hard part is whether they're still standing next to you when the hard times come.
Marriage version: marriage isn't keeping the person who makes you happiest. It's keeping the one you're willing to endure the pain with.

items.sourceNote: Queen of Tears（淚之女王），身體記得怎麼游泳比喻愛，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇有一段比喻，
適合送給「覺得自己再也不會愛了」的人：

「小時候學會游泳，
之後幾十年沒下水，
但只要再跳進去，
身體還是會浮起來。
你以為全忘了，
其實身體一直記得。

愛也一樣。
你以為自己忘了怎麼愛，
但只要真的投入，
你的心會自己想起來。」

別怕你受過傷就不會愛了，
那只是你還沒跳下水而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個經歷過很傷的分手、說「我大概不會再愛了」的朋友——告訴他不是不會，只是還沒開始
variations: 鼓勵版：你不是壞掉了，你只是還沒遇到那個讓你願意再跳下水的人
更短：你以為忘了怎麼愛，其實只是太久沒愛——心比你想的還記得

en.content:
A K-drama uses this metaphor,
perfect for anyone who thinks they'll never love again:

"We learn to swim as kids.
Then we don't touch water for decades.
But jump back in,
and the body still floats.
You think you forgot it all —
your body never did.

Love is the same.
You think you've forgotten how,
but once you really dive in,
your heart remembers on its own."

Don't fear that getting hurt means you can't love anymore.
It just means you haven't jumped back in yet.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who went through a brutal breakup and says 'I probably won't love again' — it's not that you can't, you just haven't started
variations: Encouraging version: you're not broken. You just haven't met the person who makes you want to jump back in.
Shorter: you think you forgot how to love. You've just gone too long without it. Your heart remembers more than you do.

items.sourceNote: When Life Gives You Tangerines（苦盡柑來遇見你），愛的溫度而非大小主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句，
戳中所有「在轟轟烈烈和安穩之間猶豫」的人：

「每個人都會嫁給最轟轟烈烈的那段愛嗎？
愛的大小其實沒差別，
差的是溫度。
是那個溫度，
讓我可以好好做我自己。」

我們常以為要選最濃烈的那個，
以為心跳越快越是真愛。

但有些人愛你的方式，
不是讓你失控，
是讓你終於可以放鬆下來、
做回你自己。

那種剛剛好的溫度，
才是能過一輩子的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在「很心動但很累的人」和「很安心但沒火花的人」之間猶豫的朋友——提醒他真愛看的是溫度不是溫度計衝多高
variations: 更短：心跳很快不一定是愛，可能只是緊張；真正的愛，是你在他旁邊終於能放鬆
現實版：找一個讓你能做自己的人，比找一個讓你失眠的人，能走得久很多

en.content:
A K-drama line lands hard
for anyone torn between passion and peace:

"Does everyone marry their most passionate love?
The size of the love wasn't different.
It was the temperature.
It was the temperature
that let me be myself."

We assume we should pick the most intense one,
that a faster heartbeat means truer love.

But some people love you in a way
that doesn't make you lose control —
it lets you finally relax
and be yourself again.

That just-right warmth
is the kind you can live a whole life in.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend stuck between 'exciting but exhausting' and 'safe but no spark' — real love is about temperature, not how high the thermometer spikes
variations: Shorter: a racing heart isn't always love — sometimes it's just nerves. Real love is finally being able to relax next to someone.
Reality version: someone who lets you be yourself beats someone who keeps you up at night — by a lot, over the long run.

items.sourceNote: 韓劇黑暗隧道比喻人生低潮，沒有走不出的隧道主題，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇有一句，
送給正在最黑那段路上的人：

「每個人這輩子，
都會走進一條黑暗的隧道。
長到讓你以為，
再也看不到光了。

但沒有走不出去的隧道，
世界上也沒有永遠的痛苦。」

你現在覺得撐不下去，
不是因為前面真的沒有光，
是因為你正好走在最黑的中間。

中間最黑，
是因為——
你已經走了一半了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正卡在人生低潮、覺得看不到盡頭的朋友——告訴他最黑的時候，其實代表已經走到一半了
variations: 更短：覺得最黑的時候別停，因為隧道最黑的地方，就是離出口最近的地方之一
陪伴版：我知道你現在看不到光，沒關係，那我先陪你走，光等等就到了

en.content:
A K-drama line
for anyone walking through their darkest stretch:

"Everyone passes through a dark tunnel in life.
One so long
you start to believe
you'll never see light again.

But there's no tunnel without an exit,
and no pain that lasts forever."

If you feel like you can't hold on right now,
it's not because there's no light ahead.
It's because you're standing in the darkest middle.

The middle is darkest
for one reason —
you're already halfway through.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend stuck in a low point who can't see the end — the darkest part means they're already halfway out
variations: Shorter: don't stop when it's darkest. The darkest part of the tunnel is one of the closest points to the exit.
Companion version: I know you can't see the light right now. That's okay — I'll walk with you until it shows up.

items.sourceNote: 淚之女王婚姻與留下來主題台詞，愛情不是甜言蜜語而是一起撐過，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇有一句，
戳破了所有人對愛情的幻想：

「愛不是一直甜甜的、
在耳邊講好聽的話。
愛，是兩個人一起撐過那段很痛的日子。
是選擇留下來，而不是轉身就走。」

熱戀的時候誰都會講情話，
真正的愛是——
吵完架、累到不行、什麼都不想說的那天，
你還是沒有走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個以為「不再心動就是不愛了」的朋友——愛不是一直心動，是難的時候還願意留下來
variations: 更短：會講情話的人很多，願意在最難那天還留下來的人，很少
戀人版：我沒辦法保證每天都甜甜的，但我可以保證，再難我都不會先走

en.content:
A K-drama line
that dismantles everyone's fantasy of love:

"Love isn't always sweet,
whispering pretty words in your ear.
Love is two people enduring the painful days together.
It's choosing to stay
instead of turning around and leaving."

Anyone can say sweet things in the honeymoon phase.
Real love is —
after the fight, when you're exhausted
and have nothing left to say,
you still didn't walk out.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who thinks 'no more butterflies means no more love' — love isn't constant fluttering, it's staying when things get hard
variations: Shorter: plenty of people can say sweet things. Few are willing to stay on the hardest day.
For a partner: I can't promise every day will be sweet. But I can promise I won't be the one who leaves first.

items.sourceNote: Lovely Runner跨越時空找到你主題台詞，每一世都會愛你，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇最浪漫的告白之一，
不是「我愛你」，
而是這句：

「不管是哪一世，
我都會找到你。
每一世，我都會愛上你。」

聽起來很誇張，
但它戳中的是一種很深的渴望——
不是被愛一次，
是被一個人，
用一輩子、甚至好幾輩子的決心愛著。

我們要的從來不是轟轟烈烈，
是「我認定就是你」的那份篤定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個值得你認定的人——或那個一直懷疑自己值不值得被好好愛的朋友
variations: 更短：我不要愛你一陣子，我要認定你一輩子
土味版：別人問我要找幾世的緣分，我說不用算，每一世翻到你那頁我都停下來

en.content:
One of the most romantic confessions in K-dramas
isn't "I love you."
It's this:

"No matter what lifetime it is,
I'll find you.
In every lifetime, I'll love you."

It sounds over the top,
but it hits a very deep wish —
not to be loved once,
but to be loved by someone
with the resolve of a whole life,
or even several.

What we actually want isn't fireworks.
It's the certainty of "it's you, I've decided."
en.editorNote: Send to the person worth deciding on — or the friend who keeps doubting whether they deserve to be loved well
variations: Shorter: I don't want to love you for a while. I want to be sure of you for a lifetime.
Cheesy version: Ask me how many lifetimes of fate I'm looking for? Don't bother counting — every lifetime, I stop on your page.

items.sourceNote: 今生也請多指教人生選擇主題台詞，現在是無數選擇的結果，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇有一句，
講透了「現在的你」是怎麼來的：

「所謂的人生，
每一個瞬間都塞滿了選擇。
我此刻所經歷的一切，
都是我過去做過的
無數個選擇，加起來的結果。」

所以別再說「我怎麼會走到這一步」，
你不是被命運推到這裡的，
是你自己一步一步選來的。

好消息是——
下一步，也還是你選。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個覺得人生卡住、怪命運怪環境的朋友——提醒他下一個選擇權還在自己手上
variations: 更短：你現在的生活，是過去所有選擇的總和；想換結果，先換選擇
鼓勵版：走到這裡的是過去的選擇，往哪走，還是你現在說了算

en.content:
A K-drama line
that explains exactly how 'current you' got here:

"Life is packed with choices
in every single moment.
Everything I'm living through right now
is the sum of
countless choices I made in the past."

So stop saying "how did I end up here."
Fate didn't shove you to this spot.
You chose your way here, one step at a time.

The good news —
the next step is still yours to choose.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who feels stuck and blames fate or circumstances — remind them the next choice is still in their hands
variations: Shorter: your life right now is the sum of past choices. Want a different result? Change the choices.
Encouraging version: past choices got you here. Where you go next is still yours to call.

items.sourceNote: 韓劇時間與離別主題台詞，趁變成遺憾前說出口，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇有一句，
聽完會想立刻拿起手機打電話：

「時間會走，
所以時間帶來離別，
也因此，時間總是留下遺憾。

如果你愛一個人，
現在就說吧——
趁這個忙到不行的瞬間，
還沒變成遺憾之前。」

我們總以為「以後再說」，
但「以後」這兩個字，
吃掉了人這輩子最多想說的話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總把「改天再聯絡」掛嘴邊、卻一直沒行動的朋友——提醒他現在就打那通電話
variations: 更短：想說的話別留到以後，因為「以後」常常不會來
行動版：看完這句，去傳訊息給那個你一直想聯絡卻拖著的人

en.content:
A K-drama line
that makes you want to grab your phone and call someone:

"Time moves on,
so time brings goodbyes,
and because of that, time always leaves regret.

If you love someone,
say it now —
in this impossibly busy moment,
before it turns into regret."

We always think "I'll say it later."
But that one word, "later,"
has eaten more unsaid things
than anything else in a person's life.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who always says 'let's catch up sometime' but never does — tell them to make the call now
variations: Shorter: don't save what you want to say for later — 'later' often never comes.
Action version: after reading this, go text the person you've been meaning to reach but kept putting off.

items.sourceNote: When Life Gives You Tangerines聖誕樹比喻自我價值，無論放哪都閃閃發光，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇有一句很溫柔的話，
送給那個總覺得自己不夠好的人：

「一棵樹，不管被放在哪，
都會好好做它自己。
就算這棵聖誕樹被擺在沒人來的地下室、
或一間沒人光顧的小店角落，
它一樣閃閃發光，
一樣讓人看了心動。」

你的價值，
從來不是因為「有沒有人看見」才存在。
你發光，
是因為你本來就會發光。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個覺得自己沒被看見、開始懷疑自己價值的朋友——提醒他發光是天生的，跟有沒有人看無關
variations: 更短：你會發光，不是因為有人看；沒人看的時候，你一樣亮著
鼓勵版：別因為現在沒人看見就把自己的光關掉，對的人來的時候，要看得到你

en.content:
A gentle K-drama line
for anyone who keeps feeling like they're not enough:

"A tree just does its job
no matter where you put it.
Whether a Christmas tree sits
in a basement no one visits
or a corner of a shop no one walks into,
it's always sparkling.
It still makes your heart pound."

Your worth
was never conditional on being seen.
You shine
because shining is just what you do.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who feels unseen and is starting to doubt their worth — remind them shining is innate, not dependent on an audience
variations: Shorter: you don't shine because someone's watching. When no one is, you're still lit up.
Encouraging version: don't switch off your light just because no one's looking right now. The right people need to be able to find you.

items.sourceNote: When Life Gives You Tangerines自我與愛情並存的台詞，深愛對方也深愛自己，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句台詞，
戳破了很多人對愛情的誤會：

「我是真的很愛你。
但我也一樣，很愛我自己。」

年輕的時候以為，
愛一個人就是把自己整個交出去，
對方的喜怒哀樂，變成你全部的天氣。

後來才懂，
能好好愛你的人，
是那個沒有把自己弄丟的人。
你還在，
愛才有人可以站著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在感情裡習慣把自己縮到很小、只剩對方的朋友——提醒他，先把自己接回來
variations: 更短：愛你，不代表我要弄丟我自己；我都在，這份愛才站得住
提醒版：對方再重要，也不能變成你全部的天氣；你得替自己留一塊晴天

en.content:
There's a K-drama line
that quietly corrects how a lot of us understand love:

"I do love you, deeply.
But I love myself just as deeply, too."

When you're young you think
loving someone means handing yourself over whole,
letting their moods become your entire weather.

Later you learn:
the person who can love you well
is the one who didn't lose themselves to do it.
You have to still be there
for the love to have somewhere to stand.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who shrinks themselves down to nothing in relationships until only the other person is left — remind them to bring themselves back
variations: Shorter: loving you doesn't mean losing me. The love only holds up while I'm still here.
Reminder version: no matter how much they matter, they can't become your entire weather. Keep some clear sky for yourself.

items.sourceNote: Our Unwritten Seoul人生是一本要自己填寫的筆記本，空白頁是開始不是結束，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇有一句話，
送給站在空白人生前、覺得自己卡住的人：

「我一直以為人生是一本書，
翻到最後一頁就結束。
後來才發現，
它其實是一本筆記本，
每一頁，都要我自己寫上去。

所以遇到空白的那一頁，
不是結束，
是還沒寫的開始。」

你現在的空白，
不是沒有故事，
是輪到你下筆了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛畢業、剛離職、剛分手，站在人生空白頁前慌張的朋友——告訴他空白是開始，不是結束
variations: 更短：人生不是一本有結局的書，是一本要自己寫的筆記本；空白頁是換你下筆了
鼓勵版：別怕那頁是空的，怕的應該是你以為它已經寫完了

en.content:
A K-drama line
for anyone standing in front of a blank stretch of life,
feeling stuck:

"I used to think life was a book —
you hit the last page and it's over.
Then I realized
it's actually a notebook,
and every page is one I have to fill in myself.

So when I reach a blank page,
it isn't the ending.
It's the part I haven't written yet."

The blankness you're in right now
isn't an absence of story.
It's your turn to pick up the pen.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who just graduated, just quit, or just broke up, frozen in front of a blank page of life — tell them blank means beginning, not ending
variations: Shorter: life isn't a book with an ending. It's a notebook you fill in yourself. The blank page is just your turn to write.
Encouraging version: don't be scared the page is empty. Be scared only if you thought it was already finished.

items.sourceNote: 미지의 서울奶奶安慰自責的孫女，躲避不是膽小是為了活下去，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一段奶奶的話，
專門講給那些一直罵自己「沒用」的人聽：

「鹿為了躲獅子而逃跑，
你會說牠是廢物嗎？
寄居蟹怕被吃掉而躲進殼裡，
你會說牠是膽小鬼嗎？

牠們都只是想活下去而已。
你躲起來，
也只是想活下去而已。」

你以為的逃避，
很多時候，
只是你拼了命想保護自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直怪自己「太弱、太會逃」的朋友——告訴他，想活下去從來不是罪
variations: 更短：鹿躲獅子不叫廢物，你保護自己也不叫逃避；那叫想活下去
溫柔版：你不是太膽小，你只是太想好好活著——這沒有錯

en.content:
There's a grandmother's line in a K-drama,
meant for everyone who keeps calling themselves useless:

"When a deer runs from a lion,
do you call it trash?
When a hermit crab hides in its shell
so it won't get eaten,
do you call it a coward?

They're just trying to stay alive.
You hid
because you were trying to stay alive too."

What you keep calling running away
is, so often,
just you fighting hard to protect yourself.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps blaming themselves for being 'too weak' or 'always running' — tell them wanting to survive was never a crime
variations: Shorter: a deer fleeing a lion isn't trash. You protecting yourself isn't running away. It's wanting to live.
Gentle version: you're not too much of a coward. You just wanted, badly, to stay alive — and there's nothing wrong with that.

items.sourceNote: Our Unwritten Seoul慢的一方善於等待，把等待說成一種能力，原創改編

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句很安靜的告白：

「他們說，
愛得比較多的那一個，
注定要等。

沒關係，
等待這件事，
我剛好很擅長。」

聽起來很委屈，
其實很有底氣。

願意等的人，
不是卑微，
是太確定——
確定這個人，
值得他把時間花成這個樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在默默等一個人、又怕自己看起來太卑微的朋友——告訴他，等待不是輸，是確定
variations: 更短：愛得多的人要等，沒關係，我剛好很擅長等
告白版：我不是在勉強自己等你，我是太確定你值得，才願意把時間花成這樣

en.content:
There's a very quiet confession in a K-drama:

"They say
the one who loves more
is the one who has to wait.

That's fine.
Waiting
just happens to be something I'm good at."

It sounds like a complaint.
It's actually all confidence.

The person willing to wait
isn't being small.
They're just that certain —
certain this person
is worth spending their time this way.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend quietly waiting for someone, afraid it makes them look pathetic — tell them waiting isn't losing, it's being sure
variations: Shorter: the one who loves more has to wait. That's fine — waiting is something I happen to be good at.
Confession version: I'm not forcing myself to wait for you. I'm just so sure you're worth it that I'll spend my time this way.
